**How To Apply \-** This is a career\-defining opportunity to help establish World Rugby’s presence in the United States and shape the commercial success of one of the world’s largest sporting events.


**Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion:**

World Rugby is committed to building a diverse workforce and therefore strongly encourages applications from underrepresented groups. We are committed to equality and inclusion and welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of their background. We strive to be a diverse and inclusive workplace, which reflects the global sport we want to be and where everyone can be themselves and feel they belong.

jobs.title: Chief Advancement Officer, YSPH
jobs.description:
Department: School of Public Health \- Office of the Dean
60 College Street, New Haven, Connecticut
Job ID: 136062WD
0\.00 \- 0\.00
**Description**
---------------



Working at Yale means contributing to a better tomorrow. Whether you are a current resident of our New Haven\-based community, eligible for opportunities through the New Haven Hiring Initiative, or a newcomer, interested in exploring all that Yale has to offer, your talents and contributions are welcome. Discover your opportunities at Yale!


**Overview**


Reporting to the Dean and serving on the Dean’s executive leadership team, the Chief Advancement Officer (CAO) leads all advancement functions for the Yale School of Public Health (YSPH), including fundraising, alumni engagement, and advancement operations.


The CAO is responsible for developing and executing an integrated advancement strategy that strengthens philanthropic support, deepens alumni engagement, and builds the organizational capacity to support the School’s strategic priorities. The CAO partners closely with the Dean to align advancement efforts with institutional goals and represents YSPH within university\-wide development initiatives.


This is a full\-time position that will be performed on\-site in New Haven, Connecticut, with some hybrid capacity. *Salary is competitive and commensurate with experience. The salary range for this role is $250,000\-275,000 and will include a generous benefits package.*

CONTACT


DSG \| Storbeck is managing this recruitment. Review of candidates will begin immediately and continue until the position is filled. For full consideration, please submit your CV and cover letter, electronically and in confidence, at the following link: https://talent\-profile.dsgco.com/search/v2/23304\.


Confidential nominations or referrals should be directed to YSPHAdvancement@dsgco.com.


**Key Responsibilities**


Advancement Strategy \& Institutional Leadership


* Develop and lead a comprehensive advancement strategy spanning fundraising, alumni engagement, and external relations.
* Partner with the Dean and senior leadership to define priorities and translate them into actionable fundraising and engagement plans.
* Serve as a key member of the Dean’s executive team, contributing to school\-wide strategy, planning, and resource allocation.
* Strengthen alignment between academic priorities and advancement opportunities through collaboration with faculty.

Fundraising \& Donor Engagement


* Provide strategic direction for all fundraising programs at the school, including annual, major, principal, corporate, and foundation giving.
* Maintain a small portfolio of high\-level prospects, personally leading cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship
* Oversee team members in prospect development, pipeline management, proposal development, and stewardship activities.
* Partner with Yale’s central development offices to coordinate strategy, align priorities, and advance principal and major gift opportunities as well as university\-wide development initiatives.

Alumni Affairs \& Volunteer Engagement


* Lead a comprehensive alumni engagement strategy that increases participation, strengthens relationships, and supports philanthropic growth.
* Oversee team members in the planning and execution of alumni programs, communications, and volunteer leadership structures, including boards (AYAPH), reunion activities, and advisory groups.
* Expand and engage a network of alumni leaders who serve as ambassadors and supporters of the School.
* In partnership with the Dean, steward key advisory bodies (e.g., Leadership Council, campaign committees) to inform strategy and extend the School’s reach.

Advancement Operations \& Organizational Management


* Direct all advancement operations, including systems, policies, and procedures, ensuring alignment with Yale and central development standards.
* Oversee reporting, data management, gift processing, performance tracking, and annual stewardship reporting across advancement functions.
* Manage the advancement budget and ensure effective allocation of resources.
* Continuously assess and strengthen organizational effectiveness and operational efficiency.

Team Leadership


* Lead and develop a high\-performing team across fundraising, alumni relations, and operations.
* Set clear goals, metrics, and expectations to drive performance and accountability.
* Foster a collaborative, responsive, and results\-oriented team culture.

Communications, Events \& External Relations


* Serve as a key spokesperson for advancement priorities, engaging central Yale teams, donors, alumni, and external partners.
* Partner with YSPH communications team to implement integrated messaging and engagement strategies.
* Oversee advancement\-related events, including donor and alumni engagement activities, ensuring alignment with institutional priorities.

Reporting \& Strategic Insights


* Provide regular reporting and analysis on fundraising performance, campaign progress, and alumni engagement.
* Deliver data\-driven insights to inform decision\-making by the Dean and senior leadership.

Required Skills \& Abilities:


1\. Strong strategic leadership and organizational management skills.


2\. Deep understanding of fundraising and alumni engagement practices.


3\. Excellent communication and relationship\-building abilities.


4\. Ability to operate effectively within a complex academic environment.


5\. High level of professionalism, judgment, and discretion.


**Principal Responsibilities**


1\. Responsible for planning, managing, and executing all aspects of development effort. 2\. Works closely with senior management to develop both short\- and long\-term fundraising goals and strategies, including a strategic plan for building a post\-campaign pipeline in anticipation of the next campaign. 3\. Works with staff to develop and implement plans to carry out the organization's goals and strategies, including establishing spec

jobs.title: Assistant Vice President of Development
jobs.description:
**Reports to:** President and CEO (interim); transitioning to the Laura R. Grafman Senior Vice President and Chief Advancement Officer upon appointment

**Classification:** Regular full\-time exempt

The Phoenix Symphony is Arizona's largest performing arts organization and a cornerstone of the state's cultural life. As the Symphony advances CRESCENDO: The Campaign for The Phoenix Symphony and prepares for its next chapter under Music Director Designate Paolo Bortolameolli, this is a defining opportunity to help lead the philanthropic effort that will sustain and strengthen one of Arizona's most important cultural institutions.

**Position Summary**

The Assistant Vice President of Development is a senior leader within The Phoenix Symphony's Philanthropy department and a close partner to the Chief Advancement Officer at a pivotal time for the institution. The AVP advances the Symphony's contributed revenue effort across annual giving, major and principal gifts, institutional partnerships, donor stewardship, and CRESCENDO: The Campaign for The Phoenix Symphony, and carries a personal portfolio of major and principal gift prospects and donors.

The scope of the position may evolve with the needs of the department. This is not an individual contributor role. It requires a seasoned development professional who is equally effective as a front\-line fundraiser and as a leader of philanthropy staff and programs.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Major Gifts and Principal Gift Cultivation**

* Personally cultivate, solicit, and steward a portfolio of major and principal gift prospects and donors.
* Develop individualized cultivation and solicitation strategies for high\-capacity donors.
* Prepare gift proposals, donor briefings, and stewardship materials.
* Partner with the President and CEO on donor visits, cultivation events, and solicitation strategy.
* Support Board members and volunteer leadership in their fundraising activity.

**Institutional Giving and Annual Support**

* Manage the Symphony's grant program, including corporate, foundation, and government sources.
* Oversee annual fund performance and donor retention initiatives.
* Manage institutional donor relationships and reporting requirements.
* Identify and pursue new institutional partnerships aligned with the Symphony's mission.

**CRESCENDO Campaign**

* Advance CRESCENDO: The Campaign for The Phoenix Symphony as a senior member of the Philanthropy leadership team, including through personal solicitation of campaign gifts.
* Support Campaign Cabinet engagement and coordination with campaign counsel.

**Donor Engagement and Stewardship**

* Oversee donor recognition, stewardship touchpoints, and giving society management.
* Support the annual gala and other cultivation and stewardship events.
* Ensure timely, accurate, and impact\-focused donor communications.
* Partner with Marketing and Communications on philanthropy\-related materials.

**Team and Department Leadership**

* Directly supervise assigned members of the Philanthropy team, including setting performance expectations, conducting performance reviews, and providing coaching and professional development.
* Participate in recruitment, hiring, and onboarding of Philanthropy staff as the department evolves.
* Contribute to prospect pipeline management and moves management practices across the department.
* Oversee gift processing, donor data integrity, CRM management, and gift administration.
* Support development and monitoring of the department's annual budget.
* Foster a collaborative, high\-performing team culture across the Philanthropy department and in partnership with other functions of the Symphony.

**Qualifications**

*Required*

* Seven or more years of progressive development experience, including direct major gift solicitation.
* Documented track record of closing five\- and six\-figure gifts, with experience contributing to seven\-figure solicitations.
* Campaign experience, including capital or comprehensive campaigns.
* Demonstrated ability to manage staff and multiple complex programs simultaneously.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including proposals, briefings, and Board\-level reporting.
* Proficiency with donor CRM systems and moves management practices.
* Bachelor's degree required.

*Preferred*

* Experience in an orchestra, performing arts, or major cultural institution.
* Advanced degree or CFRE certification.
* Experience working closely with a CEO, Board of Directors, and high\-capacity volunteer leadership.

**Work Environment and Physical Requirements**

This position operates in a standard office environment and requires the ability to use typical office equipment, to read printed and on\-screen materials, to communicate effectively in person and by phone, and occasionally to lift and carry materials up to 10 pounds.

**Compensation, Benefits, and Location** 

The Phoenix Symphony offers a competitive salary commensurate with experience, a comprehensive benefits package including health insurance, paid time off, retirement contributions, and support for professional development. This is a full\-time, in\-person position based in downtown Phoenix. The Assistant Vice President of Development is expected to maintain an active presence at select concerts and donor events, which will include some evening and weekend commitments.

**Applications, Nominations, and Inquiries**

Review of applications will begin immediately and will continue until the position is filled. Interested candidates are asked to submit a resume and cover letter to hr@phoenixsymphony.org. Nominations and inquiries are welcome and will be treated with the highest level of confidentiality.

Please note: We are only accepting applications from individuals seeking employment for themselves. Applications, inquiries, or submissions from third\-party recruiters, staffing agencies, or other representatives will not be considered unless specifically requested.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
At Proliance Surgeons our patients come from all walks of life — and so do we. We hire and support people from diverse backgrounds, fostering growth and development to make Proliance a great place to work. Our unique experiences and perspectives help us deliver Exceptional Outcomes, Personally Delivered.
  
We are proud to offer a comprehensive and competitive benefit and pay package including health coverage, 401k with match and profit share, PTO and more! For further details regarding Benefits and Washington State Minimum Wage details please visit our careers page at www.proliancesurgeons.com/careers. Compensation during the offer process will be determined based on factors such as compensation structure, experience, qualifications, and internal equity. Be Part of Who We Are!
  
  

Position Summary
  
The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a key member of the executive leadership team responsible for the overall operational performance, growth, and strategic execution of Proliance Surgeons. The role serves as the strategic and operational right\-hand to the CEO and bridges the gap between physician executive vision and day\-to\-day operations execution. The COO partners closely with physician leaders and the Board of Directors to advance Proliance's mission of delivering exceptional surgical and clinical care while ensuring long\-term organizational sustainability.
  
The COO provides enterprise\-wide leadership for Care Center operations, ambulatory surgery centers, ancillary services, patient experience, workforce performance, quality outcomes, value\-based care and direct to employer partnerships, IT applications, marketing and operational excellence. This leader ensures alignment across Proliance's physician\-owned and physician\-managed organization, balancing operational efficiency with physician engagement and exceptional patient care.
  
The COO serves as a strategic advisor to the CEO and Board, translating organizational priorities into measurable outcomes while fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, innovation, and continuous improvement.
  
The role reports to the CEO. The VP of Operations, Executive Director of Operations, Director of Marketing, Senior Director of IT applications, Director of Value Based Care, and other roles TBD will report to the COO.
  
Key Duties and Responsibilities
  
The key duties and responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
  
Strategic Leadership
  
* Partner with the CEO, Board of Directors, physician leadership, and executive team to develop and execute Proliance's strategic plan. Directly advise CEO on strategy and operational implications.
* Lead enterprise\-wide initiatives that support organizational growth, market competitiveness, operational alignment and excellence, and physician alignment.
* Identify emerging healthcare trends, risks, and opportunities and develop strategies to position Proliance for long\-term success.
* Drive organizational transformation initiatives with a focus on operational excellence that improve efficiencies, patient outcomes, and financial performance.


Operational Excellence
  
* Provide executive oversight for all care center, ambulatory surgery center, and ancillary operations.
* Serve as the executive operational decision\-maker for all operational pathways including clinic, ASC, and ancillaries.
* Ensure operational consistency, accountability, and performance across the organization.
* Establish key performance indicators and operational metrics to drive results and continuous improvement.
* Lead efforts to optimize workflows, enhance productivity, improve patient access, and standardize best practices.
* Promote a culture focused on quality, safety, service excellence, and operational effectiveness.


Physician Partnership and Engagement
  
* Serve as a trusted partner to physician shareholders, managing physicians, and physician leaders.
* Foster collaboration between physicians and operational leadership to achieve organizational objectives.
* In cooperation with CEO support physician recruitment, retention, engagement, and leadership development initiatives.
* Partner with the CEO to ensure operational decisions align with Proliance's physician\-led governance model.


Financial Stewardship
  
* Partner with the CFO and executive team to ensure strong financial performance across the organization.
* Drive operational initiatives that improve profitability, resource utilization, and long\-term sustainability.
* Oversee operational budget planning and execution.
* Identify opportunities for growth, cost management, and operational efficiency.


Culture
  
* Partner with CEO to foster a culture of accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement. Ensure our vision – Exceptional Outcomes, Personally Delivered, Every Day – is consistently applied to both the patient and teammate (employee) experience.
* Set clear expectations, performance goals, and development plans for leaders and staff.
* Partner with the CEO, physician owners and organizational leaders to foster a workplace where all teammates feel valued, respected, and empowered.


Leadership and Talent Development
  
* Lead, mentor, and develop senior operational leaders, at the corporate and local level. Build leadership capability and succession planning across the organization.
* Ensure ongoing delivery of leadership development training in partnership with the MSO.
* Ensure alignment between organizational goals and leadership expectations.


Quality, Compliance, and Patient Experience
  
* In partnership with the VP of Compliance ensure compliance with all applicable regulatory, accreditation, and healthcare standards.
* Champion patient safety, quality outcomes, and exceptional patient experiences.
* Promote a culture of continuous improvement and accountability for quality performance.


IT Applications
  
* Provide executive oversight of our IT applications team.
* Oversee the development and execution of the

jobs.title: Aquatics Program Director
jobs.description:
Lead the future of Aquatics at NEWLY REIMAGINED Walnut Creek Family YMCA!!
Job Description
This position is responsible for developing, organizing and implementing aquatic programs and maintaining a safe, hazard\-free aquatics environment. This position supervises staff and volunteers and may manage community and vendor agreements and relationships.


Qualifications
* Bachelor’s degree in recreation, business, human services, social services, related field or equivalent work experience.
* Two to four years of experience in program management experience, preferably in a YMCA or other nonprofit agency.
* Two years of aquatics related work experience.
* Minimum of one year of experience in a supervisory or leadership role.
* Demonstrated computer skills in a Microsoft Office or Google Workspace environment.
* Ability to direct programs through supervision of staff and volunteers, development and monitoring of budgets, marketing and public relations, program development and fund\-raising.
* Ability to establish and maintain collaborations with community organizations.
* Current Red Cross Lifeguard Certifications or equivalent \- prior to starting if hired
* Additional training to be completed within the first 30 days and maintained afterwards
**PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:**

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee:


* Is regularly required to talk, hear, sit, use their hands and fingers, to handle or feel, stand, walk, reach with arms and hands
* Is regularly required to use a computer for extended periods of time and be able to communicate using a computer and phone/smart device.
* The employee is often required to lift up to ten pounds of weight, climb or balance, and to stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl.
* Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, and the ability to adjust.
* Ability to complete the lifeguard skills prerequisite, such as swimming distances, treading water, retrieving water from a depth of at least 7 feet.

**WORK ENVIRONMENT:**  

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is occasionally exposed to moving mechanical parts and vehicles. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate to loud. This position usually works in an office, fitness, and/or aquatic oriented environment, but the mission of the organization may sometimes take them to non\-standard workplaces. This position works a standard work week, but additionally will often work evening, weekend, and additional hours to accommodate management of program activities and representation of the organization at events.


Essential Functions
**PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT/MANAGEMENT**

* Create, develop and implement new programs.
* Manage the aquatics program for the Wellmark YMCA, ensuring programs comply with all applicable YMCA standards and methods of operation and continually strive to attain the highest possible level of quality.
* Expand participation in aquatic programs.
**PROMOTION MANAGEMENT**

* Assist in the development and distribution of materials to market and promote programs.
* Conduct marketing and promotion of aquatic programs.
* Assist in the identification of target audience(s) for aquatic programs.
**FISCAL MANAGEMENT**

* Prepare and submit required budget planning and management information for the aquatic program.
* Monitor the aquatic’s budget to ensure revenue and expenses are consistent with the approved budget plan.
* Prepare and submit all required financial information and reports.
**HUMAN RESOURCE MANAGEMENT**

* Determine staffing needs of the aquatics team within YMCA guidelines and procedures.
* Manage the recruiting process and the selection process as determined by guidelines and procedures.
* Conduct required orientation and training for assigned staff.
* Manage, supervise, and evaluate assigned staff to ensure satisfactory job performance.
* Manage and maintain staffing to cover all areas of aquatics.
* Ensure applicable staff development opportunities are taken by assigned staff.
* Prepare and submit payroll paperwork and time/attendance information as required by YMCA policies and procedures and in compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
**MEMBER SERVICE/COMMUNITY RELATIONS**

* Build relationships with members connecting each person with the YMCA.
* Develop and maintain collaborative relationships with community organizations.
* Serve as a YMCA representative, both within the organization and outside the organization.
* Demonstrate and support the objectives of the organization as embodied in the mission statement and value
**OTHER**

* Assist in organization’s financial development efforts, including the annual fundraising campaign.
* Plan, prepare and organize special events as required.
* Serve on organization and community committees and teams as required.
Cause\-Driven Leadership Competencies
* Change Leadership
* Communication \& Influence
* Critical Thinking \& Decision Making
* Program / Project Management
* Developing Self \& Others
Required Certifications
Current Red Cross Lifeguard Certifications or equivalent \- prior to starting if hired

jobs.title: Director of Marketing and Communications
jobs.description:
Looking to make a difference. Be a part of a strategically focused marketing department that's helping to Innovate Within and Impact Beyond
Job Description
This position supports the work of the Y, a leading nonprofit, charitable organization committed to strengthening community through youth development, healthy living and social responsibility. The Marketing \& Communications Director at YMCA of Greater Montgomery intentionally fosters a cause\-centered culture that is welcoming, genuine, hopeful, nurturing, and determined and also builds the community’s understanding of the YMCA’s cause and impact through development and implementation of effective marketing and communications strategies.


**OUR CULTURE:**  

Our mission and core values are brought to life by our culture. In the Y, we strive to live our cause of strengthening communities with purpose and intentionality every day. We are welcoming: we are open to all. We are a place where you can belong and become. We are genuine: we value you and embrace your individuality. We are hopeful: we believe in you and your potential to become a catalyst in the world. We are nurturing: we support you in your journey to develop your full potential. We are determined: above all else, we are on a relentless quest to make our community stronger beginning with you.


Qualifications
* Bachelor's degree in related field preferred or equivalent combination of education and experience.
* YMCA Team Leader certification preferred.
* Previous professional experience in membership, marketing and/or sales preferred.
* Excellent personal computer skills and experience with standard business software.
* Must have strong interpersonal, public relations and communications skills, including the ability to make presentations and handle media inquiries.
Essential Functions
* Develops annual operating goals, objectives and plan for the marketing and communications area. Monitors the achievement of this plan, taking appropriate action to ensure that the goals and objectives are met.
* Develops, produces and distributes program information necessary to promote assigned programs, in accordance with membership and marketing plans.
* Maintains brand guidelines with educational trainings for all staff on usage of design software.
* Maintains contacts with media representatives and writes and submits press releases when appropriate. Maintains updated files of YMCA’s media relations (both press and electronic).
* Develops, monitors and administers assigned annual budget and maintains a positive fiscal position.
* Identifies and stewards relationships with internal and/or external partners or key stakeholders, such as service groups, community organizations or companies, to support programs or projects.
* Develops presentations and video marketing materials about the YMCA for events and board usage.
* Hires, trains, evaluates and supervises assigned staff and volunteers. Provides development and leadership.
* Collaborates with departments to streamline engagement and experience efforts.
* Provides and maintains related statistics and reports. Supports creation of Annual \& Impact reports.
* Educates staff, volunteers, and members about the charitable nature of the Y; leads assigned aspects of the fundraising campaign, including videos and theme designs.
* May plan and coordinate special events.
* All other duties as assigned in accordance with being an association level director.
Cause\-Driven Leadership Competencies
* Collaboration \& Relationship Building
* Brand Management \& Storytelling
* Communication \& Influence
* Innovation
Required Certifications
CPR, First Aid/AED or within 30 days of hire


Required YMCA Safety Trainings


**WORK ENVIRONMENT \& PHYSICAL DEMANDS:**  

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to use a computer for extended periods of time and be able to communicate using a computer and phone/smart device. The employee frequently is required to sit and reach, and must be able to move around the work environment. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, and the ability to adjust.
  

The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.


**The YMCA of Greater Montgomery is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Employment is subject to a background check.**

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
Description:

Full\-time: 40 hours per week


Primary Team Leader: Vice President of Development


FLSA Classification: Salary, Exempt, Full Time, Benefits Eligible


Direct Reports: Corporate Engagement Coordinator, Development Data Associate


Starting pay of $75,000 plus bonus

  

Job Summary:


The Director of Development is a strategic fundraising leader responsible for driving revenue growth across individual giving, corporate partnerships, and institutional funding. This role oversees the planning and execution of Habitat KC’s annual giving campaign, manages a portfolio of donors and partners, and leads the development of compelling proposals and grant submissions. The Director will be a data\-driven, relationship\-focused professional who can translate strategy into results, while strengthening a sustainable and diversified funding pipeline.


Primary Responsibilities:

* Direct a comprehensive annual giving strategy, overseeing segmentation, messaging, timelines, and performance to drive donor acquisition, retention, and upgrades.
* Lead the development and execution of Habitat KC’s end\-of\-year campaign in close collaboration with marketing, ensuring cohesive messaging, integrated channels, and strong performance against revenue goals.
* Manage the FreeWill partnership and oversee planned giving communications and stewardship
* Lead the growth and long\-term sustainability of individual giving across annual and mid\-level donors, building a strong pipeline for major gifts.
* Lead the expansion and sustainability of the mid\- to major\-level corporate giving strategy, including segmentation, annual revenue goals, and moves management plans.
* Oversee the strategy and execution of corporate giving, including sponsorships, partnerships, and engagement opportunities aligned with organizational priorities.
* Develop and lead the strategic growth and execution of Habitat KC’s affinity build initiatives, including Women Build, Pride Build, and Veterans Build.
* Manage a portfolio of individual, corporate, and foundation donors, with responsibility for identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship.
* Create and implement strategies to upgrade mid\-level donors into major donors across corporate and individual giving.
* Lead the research, writing, and submission of corporate grant proposals, ensuring alignment with program priorities and funding opportunities.
* Oversee the development of donor\-facing materials, including sponsorship packages, proposals, annual giving appeals, impact reports, and stewardship communications.
* Monitor performance and pipeline health across all giving streams, using data to analyze, report, and refine strategies to achieve revenue goals and ensure long\-term sustainability.


Other Development Activities:

* Collaborate with the broader Community Engagement team to strengthen a culture of philanthropy and align strategies across fundraising efforts.
* Perform other duties as assigned.


Requirements:

Qualifications, Education and Experience:

* BA/BS Degree preferred
* 5\-7 years’ experience in the field of Resource Development or equivalent combination of experience, education, and training
* Experience working with Microsoft Office
* Experience with volunteer management software and donor database software preferred.
* Active support of Habitat for Humanity of Kansas City’s values:


 o Humility \- We are part of something bigger than ourselves.


 o Courage \- We do what’s right, even when it is difficult or unpopular.


 o Accountability \- We take personal responsibility for Habitat’s mission.


Physical Requirements Necessary to Preform This Job:

* Outside meetings and presentations are frequently required
* Some evening and weekend meetings required
* Occasional special projects require working on the construction site leading volunteers or overseeing corporate donors.


The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.

jobs.title: Managing Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**

**Managing Director of Philanthropy**

One of the leading historical societies in the country, the Missouri Historical Society seeks a Managing Director of Philanthropy and Engagement who will help guide the organization into its next chapter. The Society, which operates the Missouri History Museum, Soldiers’ Memorial Military Museum, and the Library and Research Center, is at a pivotal moment. MHS is in the middle of major efforts to transform its permanent galleries, to share more of its collection than it ever has before, and to redefine the role of historical societies by establishing itself as a real civic leader in the St. Louis region.


We are looking for a dynamic leader who believes in the power of local history to strengthen communities and who will lead our fundraising efforts to support initiatives designed to build emotional connections to place; who will increase our annual giving, which averages $2\.5 to $3 million from more than 4,000 donors and members, and who will look for creative new ways to connect with established donors and to identity new ones; who will manage a team responsible for fundraising and donor cultivation and who will lead that team with a sense of vision and optimism; and who will partner with the president and the leadership team to share our mission of engaging and inspiring audiences by connecting them to their shared past and helping them use that past to better plan the present and future.


MHS has a $28 million annual budget and a staff size of 200\. We are generously supported by the Zoo Museum Tax District, which provides roughly half of our yearly funding.


**Scope of Responsibilities**


The position is responsible for leading development strategies to achieve the MHS goals for fund raising and membership as well as donor cultivation and stewardship. This includes supervision of staff to plan and implement on\-going annual, major and planned giving; and membership, as well as developing strategies when appropriate for implementation of a capital or sustained giving campaign. In addition to development and membership, the position provides cross\-departmental leadership working collaboratively with other MHS teams including Marketing and Communications, Community Outreach and Education, Public History, Collections, and Finance to achieve the strategic goals set for the MHS.


**Responsibilities for Management and Donor Stewardship**

* Provide strategic leadership for the Missouri Historical Society's philanthropy program, fostering a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization and advancing MHS's mission through sustainable fundraising strategies.
* Lead, mentor, and inspire a high\-performing philanthropy team, creating a collaborative, accountable, and supportive environment that encourages professional growth and exceptional results. The team currently includes a major gift officers, a senior charitable giving officer, a membership coordinator, philanthropy operations support, and an open special events manager position.
* In partnership with the President and Board of Trustees, develop and execute comprehensive fundraising strategies, including capital campaigns, principal and major gifts, annual giving, membership, planned giving, foundation and corporate relations, and special initiatives.
* Personally manage a focused portfolio of the organization's highest\-capacity (Tier 1\) donors and prospects, partnering closely with the President to cultivate, solicit, and steward transformational philanthropic investments.
* Provide strategic guidance, coaching, and oversight to gift officers in the identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, and long\-term engagement of individual, corporate, and foundation donors.
* Establish measurable fundraising goals and performance metrics, regularly evaluating departmental effectiveness, monitoring progress, and identifying opportunities for growth and continuous improvement.
* Lead the department's annual planning, budgeting, forecasting, and goal\-setting processes while ensuring accurate reporting and effective stewardship of philanthropic resources.
* Oversee and strengthen annual giving, membership, donor engagement, and stewardship strategies that deepen relationships and inspire continued investment in MHS.
* Serve as a visible ambassador for MHS throughout the community, cultivating relationships with donors, civic leaders, foundations, corporations, and community partners while representing the organization at key events and public engagements.
* Partner closely with the Communications and Marketing teams to develop compelling philanthropic messaging and donor communications that effectively communicate the impact of giving and elevate MHS's visibility.
* Serve as staff liaison to the Development Committee and assist the Governance \& Nominating Committee of the Board of Trustees, supporting board engagement, strengthening board philanthropy, and providing leadership in trustee recruitment, orientation, and fundraising participation.

**Education**

* A Bachelor’s degree is required; a Master’s degree in a related field is preferred. CFRE or CAP designation is preferred.

**Experience**

* A minimum of 7 years of progressive experience in fundraising, including major gifts, special events, and team leadership.
* Experience planning and executing multi\-million\-dollar fundraising campaigns with verifiable results.
* Knowledge of museum or nonprofit best practices in philanthropy.
* Proven track record of successfully closing 7 and 8\-figure gifts.
* Extensive experience serving as an institutional spokesperson and ambassador; comfortable with media appearances and formal presentations.

**Skills and Abilities**

* Demonstrated ability to establish and maintain key relationships and raise funds through annual giving, membership, major gifts, capital campaigns, planned giving, special events, and grants.
* Demonstrated proficiency with a

jobs.title: Senior Associate Director of Development, California and Hawaii Region
jobs.description:
**Job no:** 536816  

**Work type:** Officer of Administration  

**Location:** Eugene, OR, Portland, OR  

**Categories:** Administrative/Professional, Business Administration/Management, Communications/Public Relations/Marketing, Development  

**Department:**University Advancement  




**Department:** University Advancement  

**Appointment Type and Duration:** Regular, Ongoing  

**Salary:** $75,000 \- $80,000 per year  

**Compensation Band:** OS\-OA07\-Fiscal Year 2025\-2026  

**FTE:** 1\.0


#### **Application Review Begins**



Position closes July 24, 2026


#### **Special Instructions to Applicants**



Please apply using the link: AnotherSource Position: Senior Associate Director of Development, California \& Hawaii Region


#### **Department Summary**



The University of Oregon (UO) is a flagship public research institution known for its strong sense of community, innovation, and impact. Located in Eugene \- a vibrant college town surrounded by natural beauty \- UO serves more than 23,000 students and is recognized for its commitment to student success, interdisciplinary research, and public service.



University Advancement plays a critical role in advancing UO’s mission by building meaningful relationships with alumni and supporters around the world. Through strategic engagement and philanthropy, the university is fueling scholarships, academic excellence, and transformative initiatives that shape the future.



Regional Philanthropy at the University of Oregon plays a critical role in expanding the university’s reach and impact beyond campus by building meaningful relationships with alumni and donors across key geographic markets. Through a regionally focused approach, the team connects donors to opportunities that support student success, scholarships, academic programs, and university\-wide priorities. By partnering closely with campus leadership and advancement colleagues, Regional Philanthropy drives engagement and philanthropic investment that strengthens the university’s presence and impact across the Northwest and Southwest regions.


#### **Position Summary**



As the Senior Associate Director of Development, you will play a critical role in building the future pipeline of major gift donors for the University of Oregon. This is an ideal opportunity for a relationship\-driven fundraiser who enjoys identifying new prospects, growing donor engagement, and helping move individuals along the path to transformational giving.



In this role, you will manage a portfolio focused on discovery and early\-stage donors, cultivating relationships and securing gifts in the $25K–$100K range while building toward a major gift ($100K\+) portfolio. You will lead all aspects of the donor lifecycle—from identification and qualification to cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship—partnering closely with colleagues across University Advancement to align donor interests with institutional priorities.



This is a highly external\-facing role requiring frequent travel and proactive outreach. You will work across a regional model, engaging alumni, parents, and friends of the university while contributing to broader fundraising strategy and pipeline development efforts.



This role is ideal for a motivated fundraiser who thrives on building relationships, enjoys working in a fast\-paced environment, and is excited about developing donors into long\-term philanthropic partners. You will play a key role in strengthening the university’s donor pipeline while gaining experience that prepares you for future major gift leadership roles.


#### **Minimum Requirements**


* Bachelor's degree, or equivalent combination of skills and experience
* Three years of experience in fundraising, development, or a related field (e.g., sales, business development)


#### **Professional Competencies**


* Demonstrated ability to build relationships and successfully solicit gifts or close complex deals
* Strong organizational and portfolio management skills
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
* Ability to work collaboratively in a team\-oriented, matrixed environment
* Commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion
* Ability and willingness to travel frequently

#### **Preferred Qualifications**


* Experience in higher education or nonprofit fundraising
* Demonstrated success closing major gifts ($100K\+) or equivalent high\-value transactions


**FLSA Exempt:** Yes



**All offers of employment are contingent upon successful completion of a background check.**

The University of Oregon is proud to offer a robust benefits package to eligible employees, including health insurance, retirement plans, and paid time off. For more information about benefits, visit our website.



The University of Oregon is an equal\-opportunity institution committed to cultural diversity and compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act. The University encourages all qualified individuals to apply and does not discriminate on the basis of any protected status, including veteran and disability status. The University is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants and employees with disabilities. To request an accommodation in connection with the application process, please email us or call 541\-346\-5112\.



UO prohibits discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy (including pregnancy\-related conditions), age, physical or mental disability, genetic information (including family medical history), ancestry, familial status, citizenship, service in the uniformed services (as defined in federal and state law), veteran status, expunged juvenile record, and/or the use of leave protected by state or federal law in all programs, activities and employment practices as required by Title IX, other applicable laws, and policies. Retaliation is prohibited by UO policy. Questions may be referred to the

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
***Who We Are***



HOPE 4 Youth is a nonprofit organization firmly rooted in the goal of ending youth homelessness. Our mission is to provide pathways to end youth homelessness. Our vision is to ensure all youth feel safe, valued, and supported while reaching their full potential. This begins with meeting their basic needs and leads to giving them the tools to thrive. HOPE 4 Youth provides a safe place for youth to access basic needs support, educational and employment programs and opportunities, and transitional housing options. We believe in the power of a youth’s potential and work with the community to improve the challenges unhoused youth face.

  


***Why You’ll Love Working Here***



A career with HOPE 4 Youth is an opportunity to merge your talents and personal values with the desire to make a difference in our community. We need talented and passionate team members to support our mission to provide pathways to end youth homelessness. Our core values are: resourceful, accountable, inclusive, hopeful, and compassionate. As a HOPE 4 Youth employee, you will work in an environment that encourages teamwork and values the talents and perspectives of every contributor. We recognize how our organization is enhanced when we unite people of different backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives.

  


***Wages***



$120,000 \- $160,000 annual salary depending on qualifications and level of experience; In addition to salary, the Executive Director may earn discretionary incentive pay based on goal attainment.

  


***Benefits***


* Generous Paid Time Off (PTO) policies, plus eight paid holidays and two floating holidays
* Cell phone stipend
* Competitive health, dental, and vision insurance
* Long\-term disability insurance – the employer pays the premiums
* Employer\-paid basic life insurance with the option for additional coverage paid by the employee
* Voluntary benefits: Accident, hospital indemnity, critical illness, short\-term disability insurance
* HOPE 4 Youth sponsors a retirement plan with a 3% employer match and immediate vesting


Because HOPE 4 Youth is a member with Metropolitan Alliance of Connected Communities (MACC) who partners with St. Mary’s University, HOPE 4 Youth employees and their spouses are also eligible for the following professional development support with St. Mary’s University:


* Discounted tuition for Bachelor’s, Master’s, and Doctorate programs
* Professional development opportunities
* Access to advancement (internships, job placement, field practice)

  


***Job Summary***



HOPE 4 Youth is seeking an inspiring and strategic **Executive Director** to lead our next chapter of growth and impact. This is an exciting time to join HOPE 4 Youth as the organization prepares for its transition to a new Blaine facility in 2027 and continues to expand its impact on youth experiencing homelessness throughout the region.

  


**Responsibilities**


* Provide strategic leadership and organizational direction in partnership with the Board of Directors.
* Lead and develop a talented leadership team and organizational culture.
* Advance fundraising, donor stewardship, grant development, and community partnerships.
* Serve as the primary spokesperson and ambassador for HOPE 4 Youth.
* Ensure strong financial stewardship and operational excellence.
* Champion innovation, growth, and continuous improvement across programs and services.
* Build collaborative relationships with government, business, nonprofit, and community partners.

  


***What You Bring***


* Bachelor’s degree, or equivalent combination of education and experience required; advanced degree preferred.
* Minimum of 10 years of progressive leadership experience with at least 5 years in senior or executive leadership.
* Demonstrated success with leading nonprofit organizations, programs, or complex mission\-driven initiatives.
* Proven fundraising and relationship\-development experience.
* Strong financial, operational, and strategic planning capabilities.
* Experience working effectively with Boards of Directors.
* Outstanding communication and public speaking skills.
* Experience leading teams through growth, change, and organizational development.
* It is preferred if candidates have previous experience in nonprofit leadership, human services, youth development, housing, behavioral health, education, healthcare, public sector, philanthropy, and/or other mission\-driven organizations.
* A valid driver’s license, access to reliable transportation, and the ability to meet H4Y’s driving record requirements. Please note that if the license was **not** issued in the state of Minnesota, you have 60 days after becoming a Minnesota resident in which to apply for your Minnesota driver's license or permit.

  


**Candidates must submit a cover letter and resume. HOPE will not consider unsolicited candidate referrals or inquiries from recruiting agencies.**

  



Equal Opportunity Employer: All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, ancestry, religion, national origin, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, familial status, disability, age, marital status, membership or activity in a local human rights commission, status with regard to public assistance, criminal history (justice\-impacted status), housing or homelessness status, height, or weight.



Our organization has a partnership with Metropolitan Alliance of Connected Communities (MACC) to provide administrative services including management of the hiring process. If you apply for this position, you may see references to MACC in some online materials.



HOPE 4 Youth participates in E\-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I\-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S. See E\-Verify’s official poster at https://e\-verify.uscis.gov/web/media/resourcesContents/E\-Verify\_Participation\_Poster\_ES.pdf (https://e\-verify.uscis.gov/web/medi

jobs.title: Director of Development, Arizona Public Media
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**


The University of Arizona Foundation is seeking a motivated and goal\-oriented professional to join the Philanthropy and Alumni Engagement team as Director of Development (DOD) for Arizona Public Media. The successful candidate will work to secure gifts of $50,000 or more to advance the mission, strategic priorities, and public service goals of Arizona Public Media.


The DOD will be a key member of the development team reporting to the Senior Director of Development for the Unit. This position is a member of the Philanthropy and Alumni Engagement Team (PAE) and subject to joint management by the University of Arizona and the University of Arizona Foundation as specified in the restated Development Services and Asset Management Agreement dated July 2, 2021\.

**Supervisory Responsibility**


This position is not supervisory in nature.

**Duties and Responsibilities**

* At the direction of the Senior Director of Development, lead key fundraising priority initiatives for the Unit. Serve as a leadership partner with key team members and administrators on fundraising priorities and development operations initiatives.
* Develop and sustain strong relationships with Arizona Public Media leadership, community advisors, volunteers, donors, audience members, University stakeholders, and financial professionals.
* Responsible for building and maintaining a healthy portfolio defined for DODs as 50\-70 assigned prospects with 40\-50 in cultivation/solicitation; 40% of those in cultivation/solicitation capacity rated at $250K\+ and 10% rated at $1M\+.
* Develop and execute strategic, personalized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship plans for an assigned portfolio ($50,000 capacity or greater) of donor prospects (individual, corporate, and foundation) through personal meetings (in\-person or via live video conferencing platforms), letters, phone calls, email, text messaging and other methods.
* Effectively initiate and complete Qualification Plans per PAE standards to develop new major gift prospects.
* Plan, coordinate, and schedule weekly personal meetings with prospects and donors utilizing UAF software, introductory letters, emails/texts, and phone calls to set appointments.
* Provide a high level of support in stewardship and recognition efforts for AZPM members, annual fund donors, and leadership\-level supporters.
* After an initial 6\-month onboarding period, execute an agreed\-upon metric of at least 100 current or prospect donor meetings and the successful closure of 15\+ major/principal gift proposals per year, with an expectation of $1M\+ raised after the initial onboarding period (with incremental increases after the first year).
* Develop personalized strategies for all assigned donors and prospects which builds, maintains, and enhances relationships while furthering donors’ philanthropic objectives.
* Design and manage solicitation and stewardship plans in UAF CRM platform to support fundraising initiatives.
* Maintain current and accurate records of donor prospects recorded in UAF CRM platform. In a timely manner, but no less frequently than on a weekly basis, enter contact reports of all meetings, significant conversations, letters and correspondence, gift solicitations, and other communications with donors.
* Ensure all contributions are properly documented and handled in accordance with the University of Arizona Gift Policy.
* Partner with PAE alumni engagement, annual giving, membership, and community engagement colleagues to build and strengthen the donor pipeline for Arizona Public Media.
* Partner with, and effectively utilize, the development professionals and resources of PAE, while coordinating with Arizona Public Media leadership and membership teams to identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward major gift prospects and donors.
* Creatively leverage Arizona Public Media programming, community events, station tours, productions, screenings, lectures, and audience engagement opportunities to identify and cultivate new major gift prospects.
* Utilize the common systems, policies, and procedures adhered to by PAE team members.
* Support and promote a culture of philanthropy across Arizona Public Media and the University of Arizona while working effectively as part of the greater PAE team.
* Represent the Unit, University, and PAE at public events, including delivering remarks.
* Perform other related duties as assigned.
**Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities**

* Ability to understand and follow all Federal, UA, UAF and FCC policies and procedures, including UA scholarship and financial aid policies, as well as the University of Arizona Gift Policy.
* Commitment to working in a collaborative team environment with shared goals.
* Ability to work efficiently with a wide variety of individuals, including donors, audience members, volunteers, community leaders, PAE colleagues, and UA and AZPM leadership and staff.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
* Ability to effectively communicate both verbally and in writing.
* Demonstrated commitment to exceptional customer service with the ability to translate the PAE guiding principles of donor\-centricity and collaboration into work practices.
* Ability to recognize and handle highly confidential and sensitive information in a discrete and professional manner.
* Work with excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Be able to work independently with the ability to prioritize multiple projects and meet strict deadlines for deliverables.
* Ability to anticipate and discern work priorities and meet deadlines with little supervision.
* Adept at problem\-solving and using judgment in situations requiring initiative, tact, and confidentiality.
* An exceptional work ethic and track record of personal initiative required.
* Openness to new ideas and personal flexibility with the ability to adapt and succeed in a multi\-task, fast\-paced environment with changing processes and occasional ambig

jobs.title: Deputy Director of Content
jobs.description:
The JB for Governor Campaign is seeking a Deputy Director of Content for the 2026 election cycle to lead the campaign’s digital content operation. This role manages the team responsible for planning, producing, and publishing content across the campaign’s digital platforms, ensuring every piece of content supports the campaign’s messaging, priorities, and audience engagement goals.  

  

Location: Chicago, IL (this position may travel as needed)  

Reports to: Digital Director  

Salary range: $103,500 \- $111,600  

  

**Role Overview**  

The Deputy Director of Content serves as the operational lead for the campaign’s content program, building systems that keep content moving from idea to publication. This role manages content planning, team workflows, cross\-departmental coordination, and publishing priorities while mentoring staff and maintaining a high bar for quality, consistency, and execution.  

  

The ideal candidate is an exceptional writer, editor, and content strategist with strong leadership skills, sound editorial judgment, and the ability to thrive in a fast\-paced political campaign.  

  

**Key Responsibilities**

* Manage the daily execution of the campaign's content strategy.
* Develop and maintain editorial calendars across all digital channels.
* Coordinate content requests from campaign leadership and department heads.
* Oversee content production schedules and workflow management.
* Edit and review content to ensure message consistency and quality.
* Work with creative, video, and social teams to develop integrated content campaigns.
* Identify storytelling opportunities from campaign events, voter interactions, and news developments.
* Assist in developing long\-form narrative projects and documentary\-style content.
* Ensure content aligns with campaign messaging and communications priorities.
* Manage content approvals and publication timelines.
* Track content performance and recommend adjustments to improve effectiveness.
* Support rapid\-response content development during major campaign moments.
* Maintain content libraries and editorial planning documents.
* Train and mentor junior content staff and interns.
* Coordinate content production for major campaign milestones and events.
* Manage relationships with freelance writers, contractors, or external content contributors as needed.
* Support other campaign departments and initiatives as needed, including advance work, event coverage, and cross\-departmental projects during high\-priority moments.

  

**About You*** You are an experienced content strategist who can see the big picture while managing the details of daily execution.
* You have strong editorial judgment and can quickly assess whether content is on\-message, on\-brand, and ready to publish.
* You are a skilled writer and editor who leads by example and elevates the work of your team.
* You thrive in a leadership role — you enjoy mentoring junior staff, building systems, and creating structure that helps a team scale.
* You are deeply familiar with how political communications work and understand campaign messaging cycles, rapid response, and audience targeting.
* You are comfortable managing cross\-departmental relationships and navigating competing priorities from multiple stakeholders.
* You are calm under pressure and can pivot quickly when campaign priorities shift.
* You understand that campaign work often requires flexibility, including long hours and weekend work during key moments.

  

**Preferred Skills \& Qualifications**
* 5\+ years of experience in digital content strategy, editorial management, or political communications.
* Experience managing or supervising content teams, including setting priorities, providing feedback, and developing staff.
* Strong writing and editing skills with a portfolio demonstrating range across platforms and formats.
* Experience developing and managing editorial calendars and content workflows at scale.
* Deep understanding of social media platforms, content trends, and audience engagement strategies.
* Experience in political campaigns, advocacy organizations, or fast\-paced communications environments.
* Familiarity with content analytics and performance tracking tools.
* Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining quality and meeting deadlines.
* Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate across departments.

  

**How To Apply**  

Submit your resume *and* a portfolio or collection of work samples that demonstrate your editorial leadership, content strategy experience, and writing ability. This can include content calendars, campaign content you developed or oversaw, writing samples, or links to published work. Specify your role in each sample.  

  

**Applications without a resume** ***and*** **a portfolio or work samples will not be considered.** Resumes will be reviewed as they are received, and selected candidates will be contacted for an interview on a rolling basis.  

*JB for Governor is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, genetic information, protected veteran status, marital or familial status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability status, criminal record information (except where permitted under applicable law), or any other category prohibited by local, state, or federal law.*
