jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Experience Senior Living is thrilled to announce The Reserve Strathmore Square
scheduled to open in March 2028.
As development progresses, we are beginning the search for an Executive Director
who will play a critical role in pre-operations, sales, and team building during the final stages of development and through opening. This is a unique opportunity to help launch a new community and shape the resident and associate experience from the ground up.

Pre-Operations Responsibilities:

Provide executive leadership during the pre-opening phase, partnering with development, construction, marketing, and corporate teams to ensure operational readiness, regulatory compliance, and a successful community launch.
Lead and support pre-opening sales and marketing efforts, including relationship building in the local market, referral network development, and achievement of initial occupancy goals.
Recruit, onboard, and develop the foundational leadership team, establishing culture, service standards, and operational systems prior to opening.
Oversee pre-opening budgets, policies, and procedures, ensuring alignment with brand standards and a smooth transition from development to full operations

Operating Responsibilities:

Responsible for leading day-to-day operations, including full P & L responsibility.
Ensure compliance with all local, state, and federal regulations, as well as Experience Senior Living's company policies and procedures, standards, and guidelines.
Focuses on creating a safe environment for residents, families, and team members.
Ensure adherence to the Resident's Rights
Focused on resident, family, and team members satisfaction
Be able to analyze data, root causes and develop a plan of action if necessary.
Develop and implement successful strategies regarding labor, occupancy growth, revenue growth, expense control, and quality of services.
Lead morning stand up meetings and ensure minutes are taken for those who were not present.
Strong communications skills; listening, oral and written.
Lead and support all sales and operational strategies, educating all team members of the importance to be part of the sales process.
Actively partnering with the Director of Sales to conduct a quarterly SWOT, and have ongoing weekly, or at times daily discussions, on sales/marketing strategies and help support all sales efforts, including conducting tours, assisting with creative follow ups, outreach, home visits, and building positive relationships with referral partners.
Coordinating with the Director of Health and Wellness and understanding the care needs of the residents and ensuring a solid care plan is in place that encompasses all areas of that individual's care. Ensuring that residents have been assessed at the proper level of care. Ensuring that the team members are focused on supporting their independence and current abilities and only assisting in areas of true need. Being available to meet with residents, families and team members regarding any care concerns that need to be addressed.
Coordinating with the Director of Memory Care to address any challenging situations, find creative solutions, assist, and support all dementia training for new team members and ongoing training for existing team members.
Coordinate with the Director of Life Enrichment to ensure the activity calendar is robust, with a wide variety, touching on the whole person, and activities are taking place 7 days per week, including evenings.
Coordinating with the Director of Culinary to ensure menu planning has a wide variety to choose from, alternate choices are available for the residents, and assist in tasting food from meals on a regular basis.
Coordinate with the Director of Maintenance to have ongoing discussions regarding the building, areas of concern, preventative maintenance schedules, room turns completed in a timely manner, required drills being completed, and assist in any coordination for CapEx projects.
Ensure that the Employee Appreciation program is implemented and adhered to monthly.
Ensure that employee performance reviews are being completed and administered in a timely manner.
Assist in recruiting, hiring, orienting, training, supervising, and evaluating of team members.
Be involved in creating the annual operation budget and capital requirements/needs, support leadership team members in using budgetary reports, and managing their department budgets, approving expenses, providing explanations for the monthly variance report, and managing expenses as necessary.
Building high-performing teams, keeping morale high and supporting the company culture on a consistent basis.
Able to work in various positions in the community and willing and able to fill in as necessary.
Creating and implementing a ‘Manager On Duty' system to ensure that there is a leadership person in the community seven (7) days per week. Also ensuring that each person assigned to be the Manager on Duty has received the proper training and has a solid understanding of the responsibilities while in this role.
Remain active in local community activities and events. Build and maintain a solid network and resources for resident, employee, and volunteer referrals.
May drive company van, bus, or other vehicles from community to social and other destinations.
May perform other duties as needed and /or assigned

Requirements

A Bachelor's degree in business administration, healthcare administration, or a related subject is required.
Required certification for Residential Care Facilities based on state regulations
Candidates must have experience with opening a new development Senior Living community
Three (3) to five (5) years of experience in operations management with demonstrated success in meeting financial and sales goals.
A solid understanding of local, state, and federal regulations as they pertain to this community.
Demonstrated success in operating or maintaining a quality, customer service workforce.
Being willing to understand, implement and maintain a hospitality environment for residents, families, and team members.
A solid understanding of facilities management.
Experience in recruiting, onboarding, orienting, training, and retaining team members.
Possess solid performance management and leadership skills, including the ability to communicate performance expectations, coach and document performance strengths and challenges, provide direction and guidance, listen, have on-going conversations regarding performance, including implementing the company's disciplinary process if necessary, completing performance reviews in a timely manner, as well as ensuring all department performance reviews are completed in a timely manner, and providing the necessary support and feedback to all team members.
Basic knowledge of computer systems and various software platforms that the company has chosen to use.
The ability to become licensed or certified as an administrator for assisted living within a prescribed timeframe in states requiring such a license or certification.
Must have an acceptable driving record per the company motor vehicle policy
Able to influence the actions and opinions of others in a desired direction. Exhibit judgement in leading others to meet worthwhile objectives.
Able to act in solving problems while exhibiting judgement and a realistic understanding of the issue(s). Able to use reason, even when dealing with emotional, highly sensitive and contentious topics. Knowing when to handle the situation independently or include the necessary individuals to resolve the problem.
Able to define realistic, specific goals and objectives. Able to define task and deliverables necessary to meet goals.
Able to clearly present information through the spoken word. Can accurately communicate, providing the necessary level of detail even under stressful or demanding conditions.
Able to relate to routine operations in a manner that is consistent with existing solutions to problems. Conform to and communicate established policies and procedures.
Able to think creatively and independently to meet worthwhile objectives
Able to be innovative to create and generate solutions and programs.
Able to define realistic and specific goals and objectives. Able to define tasks and deliverables necessary to meet goals. Can assign resources and set priorities to meet goals.
Able to research and identify various possibilities to aid in reaching decisions necessary to meet goals.
Identifies, obtains, and effectively allocates the resources required to achieve applicable goals
Build and maintain productive, work-focused relationships with team members, residents, families, referral partners, the executive team, as well as within the community.
Able to work with people in such a manner to support the company's culture, build high-morale teams, work in a team setting to accomplish goals and get results.
Function as a role model for team members by being punctual, neat, clean, and professional in appearance.
Ability to work varied schedules to include weekends, evenings, overnights, and holidays

Benefits

We offer a full benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, STD/LTD, life and voluntary life, 401k with employer matching, paid holidays, and up to 20 days PTO in the first three years.

Compensation: $185,000 - $210,000

jobs.title: Executive Director-Climate Action Pathfinders
jobs.description:
You are a Good Fit for this Role If You ….

ð Wish to apply your leadership skills to the challenge of advancing local climate action across the U.S.
ð Are passionate about fostering “bottom-up” action that can lead to regional and national impact
ð Seek a dynamic, evolving, “start-up” work environment in the non-profit sector
ð Are energized to work alongside teammates and advisors with significant experience in large-scale systems change and social impact
ð Believes in the power of bringing people together to take collective action

About the Organization

Climate Action Pathfinders (CAP) is an early-stage non-profit initiative focused on catalyzing breakthrough results on local climate action by convening and empowering community climate leaders. At a time when federal climate leadership has stalled, the breakthroughs that matter most are emerging from communities, and CAP exists to catalyze, connect, and scale them into national impact.

CAP officially launched in March 2026 with an initial cohort of 12 participating organizations across 8 communities in 6 states across the United States. These pathfinder leaders span a diverse range of geographies, political contexts, and focus areas, from solar adoption to climate resilience, and have already delivered remarkable results, including residential solar penetration as much as three to seven times higher than their state averages.

Over the next 12-18 months, CAP’s key activities will be to:
a)      Convene leaders for structured peer learning, virtually and in person, to share case studies, surface common challenges, highlight transferable practices, and co-create innovative solutions.
b)      Support partners to make measurable progress on their climate action and resilience goals through listening and consulting support, and connections to relevant experts.
c)      Build working groups to achieve breakthrough progress on key climate outcomes such as residential solar adoption, electrification, and heat resilience.
d)      Develop a Climate Action Playbook, a living document with case studies, tools, metrics, implementation lessons, and best practices that can be shared with communities beyond the initial cohort.

Following a successful pilot, we aim to rapidly expand this program through organic growth and strategic partnerships to engage over 100 communities within 3-5 years. CAP’s strategic intent is to play a catalytic role in transforming fragmented pockets of innovation into a connected, resilient, bottom-up movement for community-driven climate action.

CAP is a strategic initiative of the Higher Ambition Leadership Alliance, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, which brings decades of experience convening pathfinder leaders and scaling breakthrough practices across complex systems. CAP was founded by three senior leaders at the Higher Ambition Leadership Alliance with experience spanning academia, business, and the nonprofit sector, including roles at Harvard Business School, McKinsey & Company, New York Stock Exchange-listed companies, and the Saving Brains initiative. It is supported by an advisory group that includes leaders from organizations such as Rockefeller, the Institute for Healthcare Improvement, and Environment America.

About the Role

The Executive Director of Climate Action Pathfinders (CAP) will lead and be responsible for the overall success of this initiative. This is a general management role, focused on managing a small staff and volunteer team, overseeing our programming with climate action leaders, and stewarding financial health. Core elements of this work include team leadership, strategic planning, project and operation management, relationship management with key partners, and fund development.

The ideal candidate will bring be able to apply their leadership skills to the challenge of convening and supporting local climate action leaders in advancing their decarbonization goals. This is an exciting opportunity to shape an early-stage initiative, work with an incredible group of local climate action leaders, and advance large-scale change on one of the greatest challenges of our time.

Salary, Benefits and Details

This is a full-time, remote position available to anyone living in and eligible to work in the United States or Canada. Occasional travel will be required within the United States. Competitive salary and benefits package will be provided based on the candidate’s prior experience.

Qualifications

We are looking for an early or mid-career professional with roughly 5-10 years of relevant experience (including relevant academic or volunteer experience).

The core qualifications we are seeking for this role are:
·      Strategic, collaborative, and values-driven leadership
·      Ability to build authentic, high-trust relationships and support partners in their goals
·      Skilled at designing and facilitating group processes to advance shared learning and collective action
·      Fundraising and financial management capacity
·      Clear interest and experience catalyzing systems-level change
·      Commitment to climate action
·      High emotional intelligence, active listening skills, and willingness to give and receive feedback
Other helpful qualifications include experience in:
Team leadership:
·      Leading teams and managing day-to-day operations in a mission-driven organization
·      Strategic planning and translating strategy into implementation
·      Being a team player with experience managing upwards and managing external partnerships
Partner engagement:
·      Advising or consulting with organizations to understand needs, surface challenges, and support implementation
·      Building partnerships and organizational collaborations
·      Working in a nonprofit, public sector, community development, environmental, or related context
·      Systems change or quality improvement initiatives
Communications, programs and policy:
·      Delivering high quality written and in-person communication
·      Managing virtual and in-person events and conferences
·      Community organizing or campaigning skills focused on environmental or social issues
·      Knowledge of local climate mitigation and adaptation practices, policy
Finance and fundraising:
·      Fundraising particularly with individual donors, high-net-worth donors, and philanthropic foundations
·      Creating budgets and tracking revenues and expenses

For more background on Climate Action Pathfinders and its staff and advisors please visit our web page:  https://www.higherambition.org/climate-action-pathfinders
Please send resume and cover letters to cap@higherambition.org with subject line “Executive Director Application”. We ask that you please combine your resume and cover letter into a single PDF file, and include your first initial and last name in the file title, e.g.: “J Doe – Resume + Cover Letter”. Short-listed candidates will be invited to interview with our team via Zoom.

Higher Ambition Leadership Alliance is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, identities, and abilities . Even if you don’t feel you meet 100% of the criteria, if you are excited about this role and believe you have relevant experiences, we would love to hear from you.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive officer of Amare and works in partnership with the Board of Directors to advance the organization's mission, strategic vision, and long-term sustainability. This position is responsible for leading a rapidly growing Recovery Community Organization through its next phase of organizational development by building systems, developing leaders, expanding community partnerships, and securing sustainable funding.
The Executive Director provides strategic leadership while ensuring operational excellence, financial stewardship, grant compliance, and exceptional peer recovery support services. The successful candidate is a visionary leader who enjoys building organizations, developing people, and creating long-term impact within the recovery community.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership & Organizational Growth

Partner with the Board of Directors to shape the future of Amare by:
Developing and implementing a long-term strategic vision that positions Amare for sustainable growth.
Building a resilient, scalable organization capable of expanding services while maintaining operational excellence.
Identifying emerging opportunities through community needs assessments, research, and best practices in recovery support services.
Leading organizational change and successfully implementing new initiatives.
Establishing measurable organizational goals and monitoring performance through data-driven decision making.
Continuously evaluating organizational capabilities and creating roadmaps for future investments and growth.

Organizational Leadership

Provide executive oversight of all organizational operations by:
Ensuring high-quality delivery of peer-led recovery support services, including recovery coaching, family recovery services, workforce development, transportation assistance, recovery community centers, sober living initiatives, and treatment referrals.
Directly supervising organizational leaders while cultivating a culture of accountability, collaboration, innovation, and continuous improvement.
Coaching, mentoring, and developing staff to maximize both professional growth and organizational effectiveness.
Building strong internal systems, policies, and procedures that support long-term organizational sustainability.

Grant Administration, Compliance & Stewardship

Provide executive leadership over Amare's diverse grant portfolio by:
Overseeing multiple federal, state, county, and foundation grants simultaneously.
Ensuring full compliance with grant agreements, contractual requirements, reporting deadlines, and program deliverables.
Reviewing and approving grant budgets, financial reports, and program performance reports prior to submission.
Monitoring participant outcomes and ensuring accurate documentation, data collection, and performance measurement.
Interpreting Notices of Funding Opportunity (NOFOs), grant agreements, and regulatory requirements to ensure organizational compliance.
Working collaboratively with leadership staff to ensure grant-funded programs achieve intended outcomes.

Financial Leadership

Provide sound financial stewardship by:
Leading the development and management of organizational budgets.
Monitoring financial performance, cash flow, and organizational sustainability.
Working closely with accounting professionals, auditors, and the Board Finance Committee.
Understanding grant budgets, allowable costs, indirect cost methodologies, and cost allocation practices.
Ensuring organizational compliance with nonprofit financial management standards and audit requirements.
Making strategic financial decisions that strengthen Amare's long-term sustainability.

Resource Development

Create and sustain diverse revenue streams by:
Leading grant development efforts, including identifying, writing, and securing competitive grant funding.
Cultivating relationships with foundations, government agencies, corporate sponsors, and private donors.
Developing fundraising strategies that diversify organizational revenue.
Strengthening Amare's brand and community presence to increase philanthropic investment.
Identifying new funding opportunities aligned with Amare's mission and strategic priorities.

Community Leadership

Serve as Amare's primary ambassador by:
Building meaningful partnerships across healthcare, behavioral health, government, education, law enforcement, employers, and community organizations.
Representing Amare through public speaking engagements, conferences, coalition meetings, media interviews, and legislative advocacy.
Cultivating strong relationships with elected officials, funders, community leaders, and partner organizations.
Promoting recovery-oriented systems of care while reducing stigma surrounding substance use disorders.

Board Relations & Governance

Partner with the Board of Directors to ensure effective governance by:
Providing timely and transparent communication regarding organizational performance, finances, strategic initiatives, and emerging risks.
Supporting Board development and strategic planning.
Assisting with policy development and governance best practices.
Ensuring compliance with all nonprofit regulations and organizational bylaws.

Ideal Candidate

The successful candidate will demonstrate:
A deep commitment to Amare's mission and the recovery community.
Executive leadership experience within nonprofit, behavioral health, healthcare, human services, or related organizations.
Five or more years of progressive leadership experience managing programs, budgets, staff, and organizational growth.
Experience overseeing grant-funded programs and complex funding portfolios.
Demonstrated success building strategic partnerships and community collaborations.
Strong financial management and budgeting experience.
Exceptional communication, public speaking, and relationship-building skills.
Proven ability to lead organizational change and build high-performing teams.
Experience supervising and developing leaders.
Proficiency with technology and data systems that improve organizational effectiveness.
Bachelor's degree required; candidates with significant executive leadership experience and demonstrated success in nonprofit management may be considered in lieu of a degree. A master's degree is preferred.
Lived experience in recovery or significant experience working within recovery support services is highly valued.

Link to our website: https://amarenfp.org/

The Ivy Mountain View
is a premier senior living community situated on a beautifully landscaped campus. Residents enjoy a rewarding lifestyle with individualized comprehensive support that promotes continuing independence. We deliver meaningful lifestyles and relationships with residents, families, and team members by developing a winning culture and living these values: Authenticity * Teamwork * Compassion * Commitment * Resilience.

Medical, Dental, and Vision benefits

Complimentary Meals
Bonus Opportunities
Company Paid Life Insurance
Team Member Discount Program (LifeMart)
401(k) Savings Plan with Company Match
Recognition Programs
Student Loan Refinancing
Pet Insurance
Employee Assistance Program
Emergency Financial Assistance

In alignment with the Company’s mission and values, the Assistant Executive Director provides critical support to the Executive Director (ED) in overseeing the daily operations and managing team members to ensure the delivery of high-quality care and services to residents. Additionally, a key strategic component is the ability to learn from the ED and other leadership to develop into an ED role in the future. All responsibilities must be completed in accordance with Company policies and procedures, current standards, guidelines, and regulations.

Responsibilities

Perform administrative duties for the Community, including recruiting, background checks, payroll, maintenance of personnel files, employee orientation, and benefits.

Oakmont Management Group,

At OMG, we strive to create an atmosphere of family and community among team members, residents, and resident family members. We know that caring and meaningful relationships are the foundation of a rewarding life, and our team is hand-selected for their skills, previous experience, and passion for working with the elderly. Our practice is to incorporate joy and laughter alongside our expectations of excellence. Walk into our communities and feel our pride of ownership and commitment to service.

jobs.title: Executive Director, EastCare
jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Executive Director of EastCare is responsible for the strategic leadership, operational oversight, and financial performance of the air and ground transport division of the health system. This role ensures the delivery of safe, high-quality, patient-centered transport services across all modalities, including rotor-wing and ground ambulance operations. The Executive Director collaborates with clinical, operational, and administrative leaders to align transport services with organizational goals, regulatory requirements, and community needs.

ECU Health is a mission-driven, 1,708 bed health system that annually serves more than 1.4 million people in 29 eastern North Carolina counties. The not-for-profit system is comprised of more than 13,500 team members, nine hospitals. ECU Health Medical Center (ECUHMC) serves as the teaching hospital for the Brody School of Medicine at East Carolina University. ECU Health serves 29 counties in eastern North Carolina with a population of 1.4 million people. On an annual basis, it serves 45,000 inpatients and over 170,000 outpatients (including 117,000 ED visits). ECUHMC currently trains 2,500 students in a variety of fields including nursing and allied health.

Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership:

Develop and execute a comprehensive strategic plan for air and ground transport services aligned with the mission and vision of the health system.

Identify growth opportunities, partnerships, and service expansions.

Lead program development initiatives, including new base locations or service lines.

Operational Oversight

Oversee daily operations of air and ground transport services to ensure efficiency, reliability, and clinical excellence.

Ensure compliance with all federal, state, and local regulations, including aviation and EMS standards.

Establish and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to safety, response times, patient outcomes, and customer satisfaction.

Monitor and ensure accountability of vendor(s) with contract and performance terms.

Clinical And Quality Assurance

Partner with medical directors and clinical leadership to ensure high standards of patient care.

Support continuous quality improvement (CQI) initiatives and patient safety programs.

Ensure adherence to clinical protocols, accreditation standards, and best practices.

Financial Management

Develop and manage annual operating and capital budgets.

Monitor financial performance, including revenue cycle, cost control, and profitability.

Identify opportunities for operational efficiencies and cost savings.

Work collaboratively with ECU Health leadership to negotiate optimal contract terms within vendor relationships.

Regulatory And Compliance

Maintain compliance with accrediting bodies (e.g., CAMTS, state EMS agencies, FAA regulations where applicable).

Ensure readiness for inspections, audits, and certifications.

Oversee risk management and safety programs.

Leadership And Workforce Development

Provide leadership, mentorship, and development for management and frontline staff.

Foster a culture of safety, accountability, and continuous improvement.

Oversee recruitment, retention, training, professional development and engagement strategies.

Collaboration And Stakeholder Engagement

Collaborate with hospital leadership, emergency departments, trauma services, and regional partners.

Represent the transport division in internal committees and external organizations.

Build relationships with community stakeholders, EMS agencies, and referring facilities.

Safety And Risk Management

Promote a strong culture of safety across all operations.

Oversee aviation safety (if applicable) and ground transport safety programs.

Ensure incident reporting, investigation, and corrective action processes are effective.

Minimum Requirements

Minimum Position Requirements:

Bachelors degree in Healthcare Administration, Business Administration, Nursing, Aviation Management, or a related field.

Minimum of 7 to 10 years of progressive leadership experience in air medical transport, EMS, healthcare operations, or aviation services.

Demonstrated experience managing complex operations with multiple service lines.

Strong knowledge of regulatory requirements related to EMS, aviation and healthcare operations.

Proven financial management and budgeting experience.

Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.

Ability to work collaboratively across multidisciplinary teams.

________________________________________

Preferred Requirements

Masters degree (e.g., MHA, MBA, MSN, or related field).

Current or prior licensure/certification in a clinical discipline (e.g., RN, paramedic, respiratory therapist) or aviation credential.

Experience with accredited air medical programs (e.g., CAMTS accreditation).

Knowledge of aviation operations, including FAA regulations and flight program management.

Experience in a large, integrated health system or multi-site transport program.

Certification in healthcare or EMS leadership (e.g., CMTE, FP-C, CCP-C, or equivalent).

Strong background in quality improvement methodologies (e.g., Lean, Six Sigma).

________________________________________

Work Environment & Additional Expectations

Does require travel between bases, hospitals, and regional locations.

Availability for on-call or emergency response leadership as needed.

Ability to function in a high-stress, fast-paced environment with critical decision-making responsibilities.

Monday - Friday 8:00am - 5:00pm

Pay Range

$143,041.60 - $208,494.00

ECU Health

About ECU Health

ECU Health is a mission-driven, 1,708-bed academic health care system serving more than 1.4 million people in 29 eastern North Carolina counties. The not-for-profit system is comprised of 13,000 team members, nine hospitals and a physician group that encompasses over 1,100 academic and community providers practicing in over 180 primary and specialty clinics located in more than 130 locations.

The flagship ECU Health Medical Center, a Level I Trauma Center, and ECU Health Maynard Children’s Hospital serve as the primary teaching hospitals for the Brody School of Medicine at East Carolina University. ECU Health and the Brody School of Medicine share a combined academic mission to improve the health and well-being of eastern North Carolina through patient care, education and research.

jobs.title: EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, PATIENT CARE SERVICES - CRITICAL CARE
jobs.description:
Summary

SUMMARY:

The Executive Director of Patient Care Services will oversee Critical Care service lines at Sinai Hospital of Baltimore. The PCS Executive Director is a dynamic and seasoned leader responsible for overseeing the delivery of high-quality patient care across multiple departments, patient populations, and service lines at Sinai Hospital. The position combines strategic leadership with operational management, focusing on patient outcomes, staff recruitment and retention, regulatory compliance, and efficient resource allocation.

Leadership & Strategic Oversight

Lead the development and execution of patient care strategies in alignment with the organization’s mission and overall objectives.
Develop and implement strategic goals, objectives, and policies to enhance operational efficiency, quality of care, and patient safety across all covered areas.
Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and continuous improvement across Patient Care Services.
Work closely with other hospital leadership teams to align patient care services with organizational goals and objectives.

Clinical Operations Management

Oversee the day-to-day operations of patient care units, ensuring adherence to best practices, policies, and protocols in collaboration with patient care services nurse leaders.
Implement and monitor clinical processes to ensure the highest standards of care across all departments.
Maintain a focus on optimizing operational efficiency, nursing productivity and patient flow.
Develop and manage initiatives to improve patient care processes, enhance patient outcomes, and reduce variability and costs.

Staff Management & Development

Provide leadership, mentoring, and professional development opportunities to nursing and patient care staff.
Oversee recruitment, training, and retention strategies to ensure the department is staffed with highly skilled professionals.
Implement performance appraisals, staff evaluations, and address any performance concerns through a clear and effective process.

Financial & Resource Management

Develop and manage budgets for all patient care departments, ensuring cost-effective use of resources while maintaining high-quality standards.
Analyze financial reports and make data-driven decisions to improve cost efficiency while adhering to quality standards.
Optimize resource allocation, ensuring that both staffing levels and material resources are aligned with patient demand and operational needs.

Quality & Compliance

Ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements, including Joint Commission standards, OSHA, HIPAA, and any applicable local/state/national guidelines.
Develop and oversee the implementation of quality improvement initiatives and patient safety protocols.
Regularly assess clinical outcomes and patient satisfaction data, acting as necessary to achieve the organizational goal of zero harm.

Collaboration & Communication

Foster a collaborative environment among clinical, operational, and administrative teams to ensure integrated care delivery.
Maintain effective communication channels with patients, families, and staff to address concerns, improve patient experiences, and resolve conflicts.
Communicate effectively with multidisciplinary teams, addressing any operational challenges and facilitating effective problem-solving.

Qualifications And Requirements

Minimum of 7 years of progressive leadership experience, ideally with a focus in critical care services.
Master's degree required; DNP preferred.
Registered Nurse License - Current Maryland license or eligibility to obtain Maryland license.
AHA Basic Life Support (BLS).
Strong knowledge of processes, safety protocols, and regulatory requirements.

Additional Information

Who We Are:

LifeBridge Health is a dynamic, purpose-driven health system redefining care delivery across the mid-Atlantic and beyond, anchored by our mission to “improve the health of people in the communities we serve.” Join us to advance health access, elevate patient experiences, and contribute to a system that values bold ideas and community-centered care.

What We Offer

Impact: Join a team that values innovation and outcomes, delivering life-saving care to our youngest and most vulnerable patients.

Growth : Opportunities for professional development, including tuition reimbursement and developing foundational skills for neonatal critical care leadership and advanced certification.

Support: A culture of collaboration with resources like unit-based practice councils and advanced clinical education support — improving both workflow efficiency and patient outcomes and allowing you to work at the top of your license.

Benefits : Competitive compensation (additional compensation such as overtime, shift differentials, premium pay, and bonuses may apply depending on job), comprehensive health plans, free parking, and wellness programs.

Why LifeBridge Health?

With over 14,000 employees, 130 care locations, and two million annual patient encounters, we combine strategic growth, innovation, and deep community commitment to deliver exceptional care anchored by five leading centers in the Baltimore region: Sinai Hospital of Baltimore, Grace Medical Center, Northwest Hospital, Carroll Hospital, and Levindale Hebrew Geriatric Center and Hospital.

Our organization thrives on a culture of CARE BRAVELY—where compassion, courage, and urgency drive every decision, empowering teams to shape the future of healthcare.

LifeBridge Health complies with applicable Federal civil rights laws and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, disability, sex or sexual orientation and gender identity/expression. jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Salary Range:
$110-120k + Equity Eligibility

Benefits:
FREE employee-only medical coverage, a 4% employer 401(k) match, and FREE short-term disability. Other incredible benefits HERE.

Location:
Chicago, IL or Houston, TX

Reports to:
Chief Experience Officer

Mission

The Director of Operations is responsible for empowering host teams to deliver exceptional, client-centered services that foster connection, well-being, and longevity, ensuring high-quality experiences at scale. This role plays a key part in sustaining and scaling Playbook’s site operations across our core service lines: fitness center management, hosted meetings and events, tenant and public programming, food & beverage operations, and concierge services. The right person will speak the language of operational rigor, but understands the soul of premium hospitality.

Lasting Impact

As a result of your time in this role, you have helped shape and define the future of work. You have enabled our host teams to do what they do best: spark connection, enhance well-being, and unlock productivity for thousands of people in office spaces across the country. You led Playbook to become a lasting fixture in commercial office buildings.

Outcomes You’ll Deliver

Operational Architecture & Systems

Translate complex business strategies into clear operating standards (SOPs), financial models, and tech-enabled workflows.

SOP & Strategy Translation: Convert strategic initiatives and programming into standardized SOPs, training materials, and resources for Client Delivery teams
Financial & Scalability Modeling: Ensure all new initiatives have a clear financial model and a scalable delivery plan before deployment to support revenue-generating strategies across diverse amenity spaces
Playbook OS Documentation: Own the creation and upkeep of operational resources, user guides, and SOPs for our proprietary technology platform, Playbook OS
Product & Engineering Collaboration: Act as a key operational partner to the Product and Engineering teams, supporting new product updates, feature rollouts, and front-line feedback loops
Tech Stack Ownership: Own the overall operational configuration of the tech stack to ensure smooth day-to-day site workflows
Team Leadership & Collectives

Oversee and guide cross-functional team members and/or committees of subject matter experts in Meetings and Events, Programming, and Wellness.

People Management: Lead and coach the Director of Wellness, ensuring their specialized strategy successfully translates to site-level execution
Collective Management: Facilitate the workflow for the Meetings/Events, Programming, and Wellness Collectives. Turn their subject matter expertise into standardized, repeatable programs
Strategic Alignment: Ensures Collectives’ outputs are aligned with business goals and support scalability of host teams
Gated Rollouts: Lead pilots and beta tests from site selection to post-mortem. Do not move to a "Full Rollout" until a pilot has proven its ROI and operational feasibility
Learning & Development (L&D)

Own the end-to-end training lifecycle for Host teams, ensuring they are masters of our brand standards, service protocols, and proprietary technology.

Operational & Technical Training: Lead trainings for Client Delivery teams on operational functions, including Playbook OS, Site Dashboards, P&Ls, and Budgets
Service Line Training: Facilitate ongoing training for Host teams on core service lines, leveraging subject matter expertise where appropriate
Onboarding & Tool Provisioning: Manage the technical and operational onboarding for new hires, ensuring they are fully trained, set up, and configured on Playbook OS and necessary applications
Knowledge Management: Maintain a centralized, "single source of truth" library for all SOPs, training videos, and resource guides. Ensure no outdated materials are in circulation
Performance Remediation: Partner with Client Operations Managers to identify specific sites or individuals needing additional training based on performance data. Deliver structured feedback reports and support remediation plans if a site fails QA
Proactive Feedback Loops: Actively monitor support tickets, Slack channels, and member surveys. Look for technical or operational patterns that indicate a need for a process change or a targeted training update
Success Metrics: Establish metrics for new initiatives to determine if they are meeting intended revenue or engagement targets, going beyond simple adoption rates
Team Communications

Drive the successful rollout of new initiatives, ensuring high adoption rates through effective project management and structured communication.

Roadmap Calendar Ownership: Own the strategic Roadmap Calendar, managing what gets rolled out and when to prevent initiative fatigue or overload on Host teams
Interdepartmental Bridge: Manage the roadmap communication pipeline between the Advisory team and the Client Delivery team
Monthly Host Meetings: Own the monthly host team meeting as the main vehicle for communicating new initiatives and the upcoming calendar. Solicit input from other stakeholders and ensure the meeting content is balanced
Weekly Cadence: Manage weekly communication updates to host teams
Support Channels: Own and moderate host team support channels within Slack to maintain clear lines of daily communication
People Team Collaboration: Align with People Team to incorporate Mission and Culture related messaging and L&D sessions into relevant host communications

Requirements

5-7+ years of progressive operational leadership experience in premium hospitality, amenity management, wellness services, or related fields. At least 2-3 years managing multi-site portfolios or regional operations
Demonstrated success executing large-scale rollouts, change management strategies, and end-to-end learning and development (L&D) programs across a distributed footprint
High tech-literacy and systems fluency. While advanced proficiency in G Suite (sheets, decks, dashboards) is required, you must also be highly comfortable learning, documenting, and teaching proprietary software, collaborating with product teams, and leveraging tools like Slack and ticketing systems to optimize workflow
Strong financial acumen. Demonstrated proficiency in creating proformas, financial models, and analyzing P&L statements

Core Competencies

A strategic thinker who elevates company performance. You have strategically moved the needle for past organizations by seeing the big picture, understanding the interconnectedness of decisions and activities, and using that insight to drive revenue and exceed your goals.
A hyper-organized ball juggler. You stay extremely organized and are constantly reprioritizing your own work because it’s what makes you most effective. Everyone refers to you as a stellar multi-tasker
Analytical and solution-oriented mindset. You are an inherently inquisitive problem-solver who consistently identifies root causes, develops actionable strategies independently, and drives them to resolution. You have a proven ability to tackle complex challenges, optimize existing workflows, and implement efficient, long-term fixes
Clear and concise communicator. Demonstrates exceptional interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills, effectively conveying operational goals and strategies to diverse teams, levels of the organization, and external stakeholders
Ability to coach and influence. Strong ability to coach and inspire others toward achieving desired outcomes. Uses a supportive and guidance-driven approach to foster growth, drive change, and achieve measurable results across teams
Silo-breaking collaborator. You proactively engage with external teams and departments to understand their unique constraints, foster mutual accountability, and co-develop integrated solutions. You focus on building alignment to ensure that cross-departmental workflows are smooth, efficient, and scalable

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: President & Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Why Churchstreeter and Crosse:
Churchstreeter and Crosse is redefining healthcare consulting by combining executive operational expertise, workforce strategy, and advanced AI technology into one integrated partnership model. Our executive leaders work alongside healthcare organizations—not as outside consultants, but as trusted strategic partners committed to measurable outcomes.

In this position...
. We are seeking an accomplished healthcare executive to serve as President. This executive will partner directly with the Chief Executive Officer to lead enterprise operations, client delivery, growth strategy, and organizational performance while ensuring the exceptional execution of all consulting, talent, technology, and operational services. This is not a traditional corporate operations position. The President serves as both an executive consultant and operational leader, partnering directly with C-suite executives across the country to solve complex workforce and operational challenges

Job Summary

The President will be responsible for transforming strategy into execution across the organization while serving as the executive sponsor for health system partnerships. This leader must possess deep operational credibility within large integrated healthcare systems and have successfully led complex, multi-site organizations through transformation, growth, workforce stabilization, operational improvement, mergers, acquisitions, or system integration.
The President serves as both an executive consultant and operational leader, partnering directly with C-suite executives across the country to solve complex workforce and operational challenges.

Key Responsibilities

Executive Leadership
: Partner with the CEO to develop and execute the organization's strategic vision, oversee enterprise-wide operations, drive operational excellence and organizational performance, ensure achievement of financial and client success metrics, build scalable business processes, and develop leadership succession plans to support long-term growth.
Client Executive Leadership
: Serve as the executive sponsor for strategic healthcare partnerships by building trusted relationships with executive leaders and boards, providing consultative guidance on healthcare operations, workforce strategy, and organizational transformation, while leading executive governance meetings and presenting strategic recommendations that drive client success and organizational performance.
Operational Consulting
: Provide executive oversight for healthcare consulting engagements by leading strategic initiatives in healthcare operations, workforce transformation, talent acquisition and retention, organizational development, service line optimization, change management, executive coaching, performance improvement, and workforce analytics to achieve measurable operational and organizational outcomes.
Business Operations
: Oversee consulting delivery teams to ensure high-quality, evidence-based client engagements, collaborate with technology leaders to advance AI and digital workforce initiatives, monitor client satisfaction and executive relationships, and drive continuous improvement across consulting methodologies and operational processes.
Business Development:
Support business growth by contributing to executive sales presentations, strategic proposal development, and expansion initiatives, while representing Churchstreeter and Crosse at executive meetings, industry conferences, and events to strengthen client relationships and expand market presence.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree required. Master’s degree is preferred.
Minimum 15 years of progressive healthcare leadership experience.
Minimum 10 years in executive healthcare operations.
Prior experience as one or more of the following IS A MUST: President, Chief Operating Officer (COO), Senior Vice President of Operations, Vice President of Operations, Regional COO, or Division COO
Experience within large integrated healthcare systems
Demonstrated responsibility for multi-hospital operations
Experience leading large-scale organizational transformation
Strong preference for candidates possessing one or more of the following: Registered Nurse (RN), Clinical leadership background, Human Resources executive experience, Chief Human Resources Officer experience, Talent acquisition transformation experience, or Consulting leadership experience
Experience working with: Academic Medical Centers, Integrated Delivery Networks, Multi-state Health Systems, Children's Hospitals, Faith-based Healthcare Organizations, or Large Nonprofit Health Systems

Work Environment

This role typically operates in a hybrid environment depending on organizational needs. Occasional travel may be required for industry events, networking, or candidate/client engagement.

jobs.title: Director of Development - Power
jobs.description:
Director of Power Development

Location
: United States (remote with travel as needed)
Overview

We are seeking a senior power development leader to build and lead a U.S. power project pipeline from the ground up- a true originator with proven experience in the utility space. This role focuses on originating, structuring, and advancing large-scale energy infrastructure projects, particularly grid-scale storage and generation.
This is not an operations, engineering, or plant management role.
This is a front-end origination and commercial leadership position responsible for creating and advancing revenue-generating opportunities.
The ideal candidate is a strategic deal maker who understands U.S. power markets, can negotiate complex commercial agreements, and has successfully advanced projects to financial close.

Key Responsibilities

• Build and lead a U.S. power development pipeline from origination through commercial maturity
• Identify and originate grid-scale storage and generation opportunities
• Structure and negotiate Power Purchase Agreements, Letters of Intent, and other offtake agreements
• Advance projects within ISO/RTO markets such as ERCOT, PJM, CAISO, MISO, and ISO-NE
• Evaluate pricing dynamics, merchant risk, and capacity market exposure
• Develop and maintain relationships with utilities, municipalities, and large power buyers
• Structure early-stage commercial frameworks to move projects toward investment readiness
• Collaborate with internal investment, finance, and execution teams to progress projects toward close

*Required Qualifications*

• 10 to 15+ years of experience in U.S. power project development
• Direct experience operating within ISO/RTO market structures
• Proven track record of negotiating and closing multiple PPAs
• Experience developing grid-scale storage and/or generation projects
• Strong network within utilities, municipalities, and large commercial power buyers
• Demonstrated ability to originate and structure early-stage energy infrastructure deals

Preferred Experience

• Infrastructure concession or public-private partnership experience
• Experience structuring power supply solutions for data centers or hyperscalers
• District energy project exposure
• Track record advancing 100MW+ projects to commercial viability

Who This Role Is For

This role is ideal for a senior development professional from an Independent Power Producer, infrastructure developer, or grid-scale storage company who has led commercial negotiations and built project pipelines.
It is not suited for candidates whose background is primarily in operations, engineering, transmission, or EPC construction without commercial origination experience.

jobs.title: Development Director, St. Luke, Shoreline
jobs.description:
St. Luke School is enthusiastically seeking a Development Director starting when the position is filled. This is a full-time position.

School Overview

Tradition meets innovation at St. Luke School, where we honor our rich history and traditions, embrace the present, and continue always to move forward. Founded in 1957, St. Luke School serves students from age three through eighth grade. St. Luke Primary Years (PYP) and Middle Years (MYP) offer co-ed programs in Preschool through Grade 8, welcoming students from around the world. We are a fully authorized International Baccalaureate World School delivering IB programming to all our students. St. Luke Mission Statement: Through inquiry and reflection, we educate globally- minded citizens who live the love of Jesus. We are inquiring minds, hearts for Jesus, and hands for service.

St. Luke School seeks a dynamic, relationship-driven Advancement Director to lead the school's fundraising, community engagement, and philanthropic initiatives. As a member of the school's leadership team, the Advancement Director serves as the chief architect of advancement efforts, fostering a culture of philanthropy while building meaningful relationships with donors, families, alumni, parishioners, volunteers, sponsors, and community partners.

Working closely with the Principal, the Advancement Director is responsible for developing and implementing strategies that support the long-term financial sustainability and mission of the school through fundraising, donor stewardship, community partnerships, special events, advancement communications, and strategic engagement initiatives. The Advancement Director should have a strong understanding of Catholic values, traditions, and educational principles. The Advancement Director plays a key role in strengthening relationships throughout the St. Luke community while helping advance collaborative opportunities that support the shared mission and long-term success of St. Luke School and its partnership with Christ the King.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in nonprofit management, business administration, marketing, communications, education, or a related field.
Minimum of five years of progressively responsible experience in fundraising, advancement, nonprofit leadership, education, marketing, communications, or a related field.
Demonstrated success in fundraising, donor relations, event management, sponsorship development, community engagement, or related work.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to engage, inspire, and build relationships with diverse stakeholders.
Experience working collaboratively with volunteers, committees, boards, and leadership teams.
Proficiency in donor management software, Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and related technology platforms. (Finalsite Enrollment, Little Green Light, Greater Giving, Vanco)
Ability to work occasional evenings and weekends as required by events and school activities.

Salary And Benefits

Salary range:
$75,000-85,000 per year. Any offered salary is determined based on internal equity, internal salary ranges, market data, applicant's skills and prior relevant experience, degrees, and certifications.

Benefits: A comprehensive package of benefits is offered including two medical plan options, dental, vision, life, accidental death and disability, long term disability, pension, 403(b), HSA, FSA, and generous sick leave, vacation, and holidays. For staff in the first 5 years of their careers, we offer the opportunity to apply for affordable, innovative housing options, based on availability.

Application process

St. Luke School remains firmly committed to a policy of equal employment opportunity. St Luke School is an equal opportunity employer following the employment guidelines of the Archdiocese of Seattle. Applicants of diverse and underrepresented backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

To apply, please complete an application in frontline and attach a resume, cover letter, and 3-5 professional references, all in PDF format, ATTN Maureen Berg, principal at St. Luke School. Upon review of the application, qualified candidates will be contacted for an interview at the school. Position will be open until filled.

jobs.title: Deputy Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
DEPUTY CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER

Lafayette Federal Credit Union

$2.1B Assets
Rockville, MD

Are We the Place for You?

Lafayette Federal Credit Union (LFCU), headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, is seeking a Deputy Chief Operating Officer (DCOO) to serve as a designated partner to the Chief Operating Officer. For more than 90 years, LFCU has supported over 57,000 members and manages more than $2.1 billion in assets, earning a strong reputation for financial strength, personalized service, and trusted member relationships.

LFCU is built on a culture of collaboration, integrity, and service excellence, with a strong focus on empowering members, strengthening communities, and creating an environment where employees can make a meaningful impact.

Our Mission Statement:

"We SERVE, SUPPORT, and EMPOWER our members by understanding their financial needs; delivering products and services to achieve their financial goals and offering solutions to assure their financial well-being."

Our Vision Statement:

"Your credit union for life."

This vision reflects our commitment to being a lifelong trusted financial partner, delivering exceptional experience and innovative solutions at every stage of our members’ financial journey.

Is This the Career for You?

The Deputy Chief Operating Officer serves as a strategic support partner to the COO, focused on strengthening execution, coordination, and follow-through across key operational priorities. This role helps ensure that enterprise initiatives move forward efficiently by organizing decision support materials, tracking commitments, and maintaining visibility into project status, risks, and dependencies. Acting as an extension of the COO when delegated, the Deputy COO facilitates communication across executive leaders and operational teams to keep priorities aligned and on schedule, without directly managing senior leaders or their departments.

In addition to day-to-day operational coordination, the role plays a key part in managing cross-functional initiatives such as process improvements, space planning, branch expansion, mergers, and enterprise projects. The Deputy COO also supports regulatory readiness by coordinating exam preparation, audit follow-ups, and documentation across internal stakeholders and external regulators.

Overall, this position is best suited for someone who thrives in structured execution, executive-level coordination, and enterprise project management, serving as a connector who turns strategy into action through organization, influence, and disciplined follow-through.

Help Us Get to Know You?

Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Finance, Management, or a related field is required, with an MBA or advanced degree preferred. The role requires 8–12 years of progressive experience in financial services, banking, credit unions, operations, project management, or similar leadership support roles. Candidates should demonstrate strong experience coordinating across multiple operational functions, including lending, servicing, branch operations, facilities, information technology, project management, and enterprise-wide strategic initiatives.

Compensation:
The starting salary range for this position is $170,000 to $220,000 annually.

DDJ Myers is the executive search consultant for this organization. Our expertise is in supporting leadership teams in sustainable practices for high-performing organizations through succession planning, leadership development programs, executive coaching, board governance and renewal, and executive search. Please learn more about us at www.ddjmyers.com .

Chief Executive Officer | Recycling and/or waste operations | Virginia

MUST HAVES

Recycling and/or waste operations, environmental sectors preferred (will consider industries such as industrial, logistics, warehousing, and/or manufacturing overseeing multi-site/multi-location.
10+ years experience in progressive executive leadership role
Direct P&L $50m
Proven experience leading business through scale up, restructuring, and/or turnaround situations.

The Company:

Operating through a global joint venture partnership, our client is rapidly growing recycling business experiencing significant growth driven by strong market demand and strategic execution. Headquarters in Virginia, and with operations in two countries and four states, they are expanding its operational footprint and infrastructure to support increasing volume and customer demand, while maintaining a focus on efficiency, environmental impact and long-term value creation. The

Opportunity:

Our client is seeking an accomplished recycling operations leader to lead a global recycling materials business unit through its next stage of growth, operational refinement, and strategic expansion. With autonomy, the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) will be responsible for the overall leadership, strategic direction and operational performance of the business. This role will oversee multiple warehouses with a focus on logistics, inventory management, reporting and environmental/health & safety compliance. Overseeing multiple recycling plants with a focus on delivering highly efficient, high quality operational performance, and development of a high performing culture.

This position will initially report to the President of the holding company during an onboarding period designed to build organizational knowledge, before transitioning to report directly to the Chairman of the Board, with two (2) direct reports.

The Requirements:
• 10–15+ years of relevant experience in an executive leadership role as a CEO, COO, President, VP of Operations, General Manager, or divisional leader with enterprise-level responsibility. Open to an executive who’s drive, ambition and hunger to win outweigh years of experience.
• Proven track record of driving growth, operational excellence, and strategic plan execution with P&L management, capital allocation and long -term business planning.
• Recycling and/or waste operations, environmental sectors are preferred. Must have a background in high volume/high velocity industries such as industrial, logistics, warehousing, and/or manufacturing while overseeing multi-state/multi-location.
• Proven experience leading businesses through scale-up, restructuring, or turnaround situations in a fast-paced environment. Proven expertise driving continuous improvement initiatives is key for this role. • Strong financial & operational acumen with the ability to drive strategic direction and align organization to successfully achieve goals/objectives.
• Experience with the following is a plus: operating in privately held, and/or sponsor-backed environments, M&A, integration, or international leadership. Experience working within large, complex organizations is strongly preferred. Knowledge of the metal production industry and market is a plus.
• High emotional intelligence with a proven ability to build respect, credibility, and trust from the BOD, senior management team, staff and external constituencies.
• Deep understanding of inventory management & security, safety, compliance, environmental responsibility with EHS a significant part, and risk management. Commercially astute, metric-driven & people focused.
• Excellent problem-solving, leadership, and decision-making skills with a collaborative demeanor. Executive presence, decisive and confident leader with strong business judgment.
• Exceptional communication, influence, and relationship-building skills with all levels of leadership. • Resilient, adaptable, and passionate about motivating others to excel through integrity, accountability, and hands-on leadership. • Commercially minded; ability to develop long-term strategic plans inclusive of capturing new business (sectors).

To view the entire position profile visit www.hohauser.com

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer [IN PERSON/RELOCATION]
jobs.description:
Yutori Method™ is managing the recruitment for this role.

This is a full-time, in-person position based in Gold River, CA.

Candidates must be able to commute daily to the office/off-site events in the local area or be open to relocation.

About the Companies

This role supports the Principal's holding companies, a portfolio of businesses spanning real estate, lending, investments, and related ventures, with Optimus Capital serving as the largest and most established operating company.

While each business has its own focus, they are united by a shared vision for growth, operational excellence, and long-term value creation. As the portfolio continues to expand, the organization is investing in the leadership infrastructure needed to create greater alignment, scalability, and execution across the enterprise.

The Chief Operating Officer will partner directly with the Principal to provide operational leadership across the broader portfolio—ensuring priorities are aligned, execution remains coordinated, and each company benefits from a cohesive strategic direction while supporting the continued growth of the overall enterprise.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to become the CEO's closest strategic operating partner and the trusted translator between a highly visionary founder and the rest of the organization.

As Chief Operating Officer, you will serve as the operational counterpart to the CEO, helping transform ideas into clear priorities, coordinated execution, and measurable business outcomes. A significant part of your role will be understanding not only what the CEO is saying, but what he means—bringing clarity to complex ideas, ensuring teams are aligned around the broader vision, and amplifying his leadership throughout the organization.

You will act as the CEO's whisperer and organizational interpreter, creating the systems, operating rhythms, and accountability that allow the business to execute with confidence while preserving the speed and creativity that make the company successful.

This role is ideal for a leader who naturally sees both the big picture and the operational details—someone who thrives on creating alignment, building organizational clarity, developing leaders, and ensuring the entire company is moving in the same direction with purpose and momentum.

Why This Role Is Exciting

Partner directly with a visionary CEO as the organization's primary operational leader
Lead the execution and alignment of multiple business entities within a growing private lending platform
Build scalable systems, processes, and operating rhythms that support long-term growth
Drive meaningful business impact through KPI visibility, financial performance, and operational excellence
Shape the organization's leadership culture by creating accountability and clarity across teams
Leverage automation, AI, and modern operational tools to improve efficiency and decision-making
Join a company positioned for continued growth with the opportunity to influence its next stage of success

The Right Fit Candidate

This role is designed for someone who naturally becomes the operational counterpart to visionary leaders. You excel at listening beneath the surface, translating evolving ideas into organizational clarity, and ensuring teams understand not only what needs to happen, but why . You create focus, alignment, and accountability, allowing the CEO to remain visionary while the organization executes with confidence.

You are someone who:
Thrives partnering with visionary founders and translating ideas into execution
Not afraid of ambiguity and change of plans
Builds trust through consistency, sound judgment, and strong communication
Leads with humility while holding teams accountable for results
Makes decisions confidently using both data and business intuition
Enjoys improving systems, workflows, and organizational effectiveness
Brings a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset to operational challenges
Is comfortable managing multiple priorities across a complex organization
Embraces technology, automation, and AI as tools for scaling operations, and can bring demonstrated use cases for success
Wants to build a long-term partnership with a growing business
Understands marketing principles and has an eye for design

What You'll Do

CEO Enablement

Serve as the primary integrator between the CEO and the organization, translating vision into structured execution
Prioritize initiatives, manage executive follow-through, and ensure decisions move forward efficiently
Protect the CEO's time by removing operational bottlenecks and improving organizational alignment
Ensure communication, delegation, and strategic priorities are clearly defined across the business

Company Operations

Lead cross-functional execution across departments, teams, and business entities
Establish operating rhythms, accountability systems, and leadership meeting cadences
Improve workflows, eliminate bottlenecks, and implement automation and AI-enabled efficiencies
Oversee operational alignment across sales, lending, processing, compliance, and corporate functions

Planning & Business Performance

Lead quarterly and annual planning alongside executive leadership
Build KPI dashboards and reporting systems that provide real-time business visibility
Translate strategic objectives into measurable execution plans
Use operational and financial data to drive decision-making and continuous improvement

Revenue & Growth

Own operational accountability tied to revenue, profitability, and organizational performance
Partner with sales leadership to improve pipeline execution and operational throughput
Lead budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and departmental accountability
Connect operational performance with financial outcomes while reducing founder dependency

Team Leadership

Develop organizational clarity around ownership, priorities, and success metrics
Foster accountability through consistent follow-up and leadership alignment
Partner with department leaders to resolve operational friction and improve collaboration
Ensure strategic decisions are effectively communicated and implemented across the organization

Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Operations, or related field required (MBA preferred)
7–10+ years of operations leadership experience, preferably within private lending, mortgage, financial services, or real estate
Proven success serving as a COO, Integrator, or senior operations executive in a growing organization
Experience partnering directly with founders or visionary CEOs
Strong understanding of operational systems, KPI reporting, financial performance, and organizational scaling
Experience with Salesforce, Monday.com (or similar), reporting dashboards, automation platforms, and AI-enabled operational tools
Experience overseeing compliance, risk management, and operational governance
Must be authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship
Valid driver’s license
Russian language (A PLUS)

Work Environment & Compensation

Location: In-person, Gold River, CA in office or local off-site events (5 days/week)
Hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST (flexibility for occasional evening events)
Expected Start Date: August 15, 2026
Salary: $200,000+ annually plus performance bonus
Relocation assistance available for the right candidate
Benefits: 401(k), Health, Dental, Vision, PTO after 90 days
Must be authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship
Reasonable accommodations available for qualified individuals with disabilities

Confidential wellness company is seeking an exceptional Chief of Staff / Executive Assistant to support the CEO — founder and CEO of a nationwide wellness company, author, podcast host, and public speaker.
This is a senior, high-trust role for someone who thrives in complex, fast-moving environments and takes pride in creating seamless operations behind the scenes. jobs.title: Managing Director of Operational Strategy
jobs.description:
Follow your passion

Are you looking to make a BIG impact? At Zeta, we pursue an unprecedented combination of high academic achievement and social-emotional development for our students. We insist that every student receives a world-class education while fostering a love for learning. We are passionate about changing the landscape of public education in New York City and beyond, and we are uncompromising in our mission.

Currently, we operate multiple elementary and middle schools in the Bronx and Upper Manhattan, and are opening three new elementary schools in Queens for the 2025-26 school year
. We plan to grow and open 1-2 schools each year because children in every community deserve excellent, equitable education. Join our growing team today, and be a change agent for children.

Our Network office is located at 401 W 218th St., New York, NY 10034. Our Network team follows a hybrid work schedule: we work in-person from our office on Tuesdays and Thursdays with optional remote days on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays, if your team's scope of work permits.

Deliver powerful results

To achieve our vision, we are searching for a Managing
Director of Operational Strategy
with a Bachelor's Degree and 10+ years of work experience in management consulting, finance, scale-up of growing organizations, or strategic roles in high-performance organizations.
MBA is highly preferred, but not required. The position title will be commensurate with candidate experience.

Ideal candidates have a creative, problem-solving mindset, disciplined focus on scalable processes, and interest in education. We are looking for teammates with a strong analytical skillset ( e.g., ability to analyze large datasets and create models) and mastery of storytelling ( e.g., ability to write for multiple audiences and create effective decks). This role reports to the Chief Operating Officer, Network.

Responsibilities:

Provide strategic visioning and project management for critical initiatives to support the network's growth plan
Design and implement seamless, effective, and scalable operational processes across all areas of the organization that support our schools, including student outreach, talent recruitment, people operations, and organizational culture and training
Lead cross-functional teams in project planning and execution, ensuring that all departments contribute effectively to the strategic scaling plans
Champion a culture of continuous improvement by encouraging feedback and ideas from all levels of the organization, integrating diverse perspectives into strategic planning and scaling efforts
Identify areas of improvements across the organization and use data, experience, and best practices to provide creative solutions
Wear multiple hats as a part of a lean scale-up team, supporting the work of others on the team and taking on new projects and tasks as needed

Join our purpose-driven community

Zeta teammates are lifelong learners who live with child-like joy and a deep sense of purpose. We disrupt the status quo and tackle problems together. Our team stands united by our commitment to a limitless future for every child. Our Zeta team is comprised of top talent with incredible schooling and private sector experience. At Zeta:

We are passionate about our mission of providing world-class education to all students
We bring a mindset of growth, flexibility, and openness to feedback
We are hungry and determined to not only meet our goals, but surpass them
We are humble, willing to put the team's needs ahead of our own, and committed to contributing to a collaborative work environment
We have a high bar for excellence and sweat over the details
We are caring and put team over individual at all times

We are hiring for an immediate opening
.
The salary range for this position is between $125,000 and $175,000. New hires can expect to be brought into the organization at a salary at or near the start of the range, depending on relevant experience and internal equity. Compensation is based on our salary scales. This position is eligible to receive an annual discretionary, merit-based performance bonus.

How does Zeta invest in you?

We offer great benefits:

Comprehensive and affordable medical, dental, and vision plans
Flexible spending accounts for eligible medical purchases and commuter expenses
403(b) retirement plan; Zeta offers matching contributions toward your savings
Competitive compensation

We provide awesome perks:

Monthly stipend for wellness and lifestyle expenses (gym memberships, salon appointments, concerts – it's up to you!)
4 live therapy sessions per month through Talkspace and free Headspace subscription
MacBook Air and specified monthly reimbursement for use of a mobile phone
Fully stocked teacher workrooms with favorite snacks and Nespresso machine
Zeta swag, team outings, and NYC's finest sweet treats
Books for professional development

We care deeply about your personal and professional growth:

Clear pathway to professional growth
Consistent feedback and support from managers and teammates
Community Circle practice to build and strengthen our sense of team
Interactive Mindfulness and DEIJ programming

Apply Now!

Equity is at the heart of our mission. We have a deep commitment to pursuing diversity and striving for equity. Zeta is an equal opportunity employer. Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Work authorization is required (candidate must have permanent authorization to work in the U.S.).
